title: fix(tci): iq_samplerate echoes requested rate instead of actual delivery rate
description: Issue #570: HandleIqSampleRate echoes the client-requested rate verbatim even though the IQ stream is always delivered at the hardware sample rate. Clients that size their DSP pipeline on the echoed value silently get the wrong rate. Path A fix: echo the hardware rate (radio.Snapshot().SampleRate) so clients detect that their request wasn't honored. Same honesty fix applied to audio_samplerate (always 48000). The stored _iqSampleRate is kept for future Path B decimation.
close_reason: HandleIqSampleRate and HandleAudioSampleRate now echo the actual delivery rate; all tests green

title: feat(cw): decode CW server-side from RxAudioAvailable, stream text over hub (headless + desktop)
description: Pivot from PR1's client-side WebAudio decoder (broken: doesn't work in desktop/Photino mode where audio is rendered natively, and can't run headless). Move decoding to the server: a CwDecoderService (BackgroundService, singleton+hosted) subscribes to DspPipelineService.RxAudioAvailable (demodulated post-AGC RX audio, ~30Hz ticks, headless-safe — fires pre-sink regardless of any audio output). Runs an envelope-based key detector (energy variation above an adaptively-tracked noise floor, with Schmitt hysteresis — works for CW-on-SSB where there is no true silence between elements) + Morse FSM, gated on RxMode.CWL/CWU. Emits decoded text + wpm/snr/confidence via a new CwDecodedTextFrame (MsgType 0x31) broadcast by StreamingHub, mirroring the existing CwEngineStatusFrame (0x30) pattern. Frontend: ws-client handler dispatches to the existing cw-decoder-store.appendChar; client decoder.ts/useCwDecoderAudio.ts deleted. RED-LIGHT: touches Zeus.Contracts wire format + adds a service → needs maintainer (Brian/EI6LF) sign-off before merge. Supersedes zeus-67t (client timing fix).
close_reason: Delivered by PR #549 (cw-decoder-548 → develop): CwDecoderService + CwAudioDecoder server-side envelope/Morse decode on RxAudioAvailable, CwDecodedTextFrame (MsgType 0x31) over StreamingHub, ws-client dispatch to cw-decoder-store, client decoder deleted. Merged to develop.

title: fix(cw): decoder timing must derive from sample count, not performance.now()
description: Live validation on feature/cw-decoder-investigation: the CW decoder only emits 1-2 element chars (E/T/I), reports a pinned ~59 WPM (ditMs clamped to the 10ms floor), and shows ~1dB SNR. Root cause: CwDecoder.process() timestamps every sample with performance.now(); processSamples() iterates an entire ~960-sample frame in a microsecond burst, so all samples in a frame share one timestamp and dit/dah durations measure ~0. Fix: thread a sample-based clock (sampleIndex/sampleRate*1000) and rework the per-sample debounce accordingly. Secondary: AdaptiveThreshold cold-start (noiseFloor=0) reports everything as keyed for the first window, emitting a spurious 'E' on every on/off toggle — add a primed guard. Deferred (#3): block-based Goertzel + slower noise-floor tracker if per-sample magnitude proves too noisy.
close_reason: Superseded by zeus-dmx: decoding moves server-side; the sample-clock timing insight ports to C#.

title: nightly macOS signing fails: missing secrets: inherit in nightly.yml
description: The Nightly workflow (.github/workflows/nightly.yml) calls release.yml as a reusable workflow (uses:) without 'secrets: inherit'. release.yml's macOS signing step (gated on is-nightly=='true') reads secrets.MACOS_CERTIFICATE / _PWD / KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD, but a reusable-workflow call only sees repo secrets when the caller inherits them — so they arrive empty and 'security import' fails with 'SecKeychainItemImport: Unable to decode the provided data' (empty base64 .p12). title: fix(protocol-1): include TxFreq in non-MOX phase rotation so HL2 extamp boards emit Kenwood CAT on dial change
description:
GitHub issue: https://github.com/Kb2uka/openhpsdr-zeus/issues/361

## Symptom

Square SDR 2 (HL2-class, fw 75.52, classifies as HermesLite2) outputs no band voltage on ACC-pin-1 BVO under Zeus, regardless of the Band Volts (C3 bit 3) checkbox state. Same hardware + same connector follows the dial fine under deskHPSDR with the checkbox in either state. Operator: Colin (@41south), driving an Xiegu XPA125B.

## Root cause

Square SDR 2's gateware enables the upstream HL2 `extamp` module, which emits Kenwood CAT (`FA000XXXXXXXX;`) on `io_uart_txd` only when the FPGA's internal TX-frequency register changes value. A daughter STM32G031 parses that CAT on USART2 and drives BVO PWM (TIM2 CH4) + BCD GPIO out to the rear-panel ACC connector.

Zeus's `PhaseRegisters(phase, mox=false, psArmed=false)` in `Zeus.Protocol1/Protocol1Client.cs:965-971` rotates only `Config / RxFreq / DriveFilter / Attenuator`. `CcRegister.TxFreq` (C0=0x02) is **never** written while receiving — only when MOX is asserted (lines 957-963). Result: the FPGA's TX-NCO register stays static during RX dial changes, no CAT edge fires, BVO never updates.

deskHPSDR's `src/old_protocol.c:2837-2846` writes `output_buffer[C0] = 0x02` (DUC frequency) on every pass of its round-robin scheduler regardless of MOX state, which is what keeps the FPGA `extamp` edge alive.

## Fix sketch

Add `CcRegister.TxFreq` to the non-MOX, non-PS-armed rotation phase table so the TX-NCO is refreshed on every dial change while receiving. Mirror to the `psArmed && !mox` table (`Protocol1Client.cs:933-951`) if not already covered.

## Risk / red-light points

The Protocol-1 round-robin is load-bearing for every supported board (Metis, Hermes, ANAN, Orion-MkII family, HL2). Before merging:

1. Verify the new rotation doesn't displace a phase a non-HL2 board depends on.
2. Cross-check against mi0bot Thetis and ramdor Thetis that they also write TX-NCO during RX.
3. Bench-test on HL2 at 28400 RX-only to confirm no regression (no spurious BVO change, no panadapter glitch, no PS-arm misbehavior).
4. PR description must flag "scheduler change — non-HL2 boards untested locally, please bench-verify before merge" for maintainer.

## Out of scope

- BCD on ACC pins 11-14 — Colin offered to test once BVO works. Fix should light up both at once (same `extamp` → STM32 chain), but bench-confirm before claiming.
- C3 bit 3 (`EnableHl2BandVolts`) wire-write — already correct per audit; no change needed.

## Test plan

- HL2 RX: panadapter on 28400, dial sweep across band; no spurious behavior.
- HL2 MOX (dummy load only, 28400): no rotation regression; PA/drive cadence unchanged.
- Code read: confirm non-HL2 boards (Metis / Hermes / Orion-MkII variant) still receive a `Config` phase often enough not to lose nrx / dither / random settings.
- Ship a pre-release build for Colin to test on Square SDR 2 + XPA125B.

title: P1: Implement onboard codec audio path (Hermes RX headphones + TX mic-in)
description:
Reported in GH #426 against Hermes on v0.8.2: 'Onboard codec not worked'. Thetis / linHPSDR / piHPSDR all work on the same board.

Root cause (confirmed in code):
- Zeus.Protocol1/ControlFrame.cs:516-532, 562 — EP2 outbound 8-byte slot is [L s16 BE][R s16 BE][I s16 BE][Q s16 BE], and the L/R audio bytes are intentionally left zero. Comment: 'HL2 has no audio codec in the MVP target, so audio bytes stay zero'.
- Zeus.Protocol1/PacketParser.cs:240 — incoming mic samples at off+6,off+7 are read but discarded ('Unused for RX MVP').

On HL2 this was acceptable because operators use host audio (browser getUserMedia + WebAudio). On Hermes / Mercury / ANAN-class the radio's onboard codec IS the primary audio path — operators expect to plug a mic into the front-panel jack and listen on the radio's headphone jack. That entire path is unimplemented.

This is the headline failure in #426 and the most likely reason linoobs's Hermes is unusable in Zeus while it works fine in Thetis.
design: Gate via BoardCapabilitiesTable. Hermes / Mercury / ANAN-class default to onboard codec; HL2 defaults to host/browser audio. TX path: route mic source samples into EP2 L/R bytes (currently always zero in ControlFrame.WriteUsbFrame). RX path: extract the 16-bit mic-monitor bytes in PacketParser.TryParsePacket and surface via an IRxCodecAudioSource seam (parallel to IRxAudioSink for DSP-out). Operator UI toggle to override the per-board default. Coordinate with audio-native plan ([[project_audio_native_plan]]).
acceptance_criteria: On a Hermes board with mic plugged into the front-panel jack, transmitted SSB audio matches Thetis behaviour. RX audio plays out the front-panel headphone jack at a usable level. Behaviour is board-gated via BoardCapabilitiesTable so HL2 default (browser mic) is preserved.
notes: Phase A landed in commit (fix(p1) on fix/426-onboard-codec branch) — RX audio reaches the radio's headphone jack on Hermes / ANAN-class / OrionMkII / G2E boards via EP2 L/R bytes. HL2 unchanged (HasOnboardCodec=false). Phase B (radio mic → WDSP TXA) tracked in new bd item — see /api/recent for that item's ID; needs operator UI for mic-source selection.

title: P2 [zeus] Bypass TX audio plugin chain (AudioChain) for TCI / remote TX audio sources
description:
Investigate + implement bypass of the entire TX audio plugin insert chain (the AudioChain / IAudioPlugin slots driven by AudioPluginBridge) when the active TX audio source is a remote TCI client (or any external/remote driver) instead of the local mic.

## Why
When a TCI client (ExpertSDR3, MSHV, remote tunnel users, etc.) supplies TX audio via the TCI binary stream, that audio has already been processed (or deliberately left clean) on the client side. Re-running the local Audio Suite plugins (EQ, compressor, noise gate, VST effects, etc.) produces double-processing, wrong timbre for voice, and broken behaviour for digital modes.

Today the TX plugin seam is source-agnostic: every block that reaches WdspDspEngine.ProcessTxBlock goes through the installed TxAudioBlockHandler → AudioChain, regardless of whether it came from browser mic, NativeMicCapture, WAV playback, or TciTxAudioReceiver.

## Current state (investigation summary)
- All TX audio sources funnel through TxAudioIngest (OnMicPcmBytesFromTci / FromMic / FromWav) with 500 ms hysteresis to keep sources from mixing.
- TxAudioIngest calls engine.ProcessTxBlock → WdspDspEngine (Zeus.Dsp/Wdsp/WdspDspEngine.cs:2417) does the volatile read of _txAudioPluginHandler and invokes AudioPluginBridge.Process if present.
- The handler always runs the _chain (TX AudioChain) before fexchange2. The chain only respects the global MasterBypassed + per-slot bypass today.
- TCI source awareness exists: TciSession._txSourceIsTci (set on TRX:... ,tci; command, cleared on plain trx or MOX fall). Exposed as TxSourceIsTci. Used only for TX_CHRONO pacing and accepting audio frames in that session. Not visible to the audio ingest or plugin bridge.
- Existing bypass (AudioChain.MasterBypassed via AudioChainMasterBypassService) is efficient (volatile + straight copy, no allocs, realtime safe) but is the operator's "Audio Suite" master toggle — not policy-driven by source.

See detailed findings in the session transcript for the investigation (key files: TxAudioIngest.cs, AudioPluginBridge.cs (the _chain vs _rxChain split), WdspDspEngine.cs:ProcessTxBlock + SetTxAudioPluginHandler, AudioChain.cs in Zeus.Plugins.Host, TciSession.cs:943 (the source flag), TciTxAudioReceiver.cs).

This is the TX-side counterpart to the RX post-demod plugin seam (zeus-3ar / feat/rx-audio-plugin-seam).

## Red-light / hot-path note
- Touches the TX audio hot path and the plugin seam (WdspDspEngine + DspPipelineService adjacent paths + AudioPluginBridge).
- Per CLAUDE.md + CONTRIBUTING.md hot paths section + the zeus-3ar issue itself: this is red-light. Architecture + signal-routing change. Requires maintainer sign-off before merge. Do not land without explicit approval.
- Do not touch PureSignal paths or the recently-shipped audio dropout scheduling without separate approval.

## Design options (record decisions here)
1. Automatic override of master bypass when TCI source is active (simple, reuses everything).
2. Separate volatile flag on AudioChain (ExternalSourceBypass / BypassForRemote) composed with MasterBypassed. Cleanest policy vs. operator intent.
3. Swap the installed TxAudioBlockHandler to a pure passthrough when TCI source takes over.
4. Propagate source information (or "applyUserPlugins" bool) down to the handler / AudioBlockContext so the chain (or individual plugins) can decide.

Distinguish insert chain (the thing we want to bypass for remote) vs. ITxAudioTapPlugin taps (recorders etc. probably still want to observe raw + air audio).

Prefer option 2 or 3 for minimal pollution of the operator's master-bypass state.

The bypass must be bit-identical pass-through when engaged (same requirement as the existing master bypass ADR).

## Scope
- Make the bypass automatic / policy-driven when the authoritative TX source is TCI (or future "remote" sources).
- Keep the operator's Audio Suite master bypass as an independent override (or document the interaction clearly).
- Surface the state somewhere (capability? diagnostic? UI hint when a TCI session is driving TX?).
- Preserve behaviour for local mic and for WAV playback (WAV is "local" in intent).
- Taps vs. inserts: be explicit.
- Tests: unit tests around AudioChain + a source-aware path in TxAudioIngest / bridge (similar to existing AudioPluginBridgeProcessLivePreviewTests and AudioChainTests).
- On-air: verify on HL2 (user rig) that local mic still gets plugins, TCI-driven TX does not.
- Docs: update relevant lesson or TCI docs if the behaviour becomes user-visible.

## Non-goals (for this issue)
- Changing WDSP internal TXA stages.
- Adding new plugins or the UI for this bypass.
- Remote TCI control surface work (that's the tunnel epic).
- Altering the RX seam.

## References
- Current branch at investigation time: feat/rx-audio-plugin-seam (zeus-3ar)
- TCI TX audio path: Tci/TciTxAudioReceiver.cs, TxAudioIngest.cs:201 (OnMicPcmBytesFromTci)
- Plugin seam: AudioPluginBridge.cs:210 (SetTx...), 225 (the Process that calls chain), WdspDspEngine.cs:2417
- Bypass impl: AudioChain.cs:144 (if MasterBypassed copy), AudioChainMasterBypassService.cs
- TCI source flag: TciSession.cs:86, 192 (TxSourceIsTci), 943 (where it is set from the TRX command)
design: Options 1-4 recorded in description. Prefer separate ExternalSourceBypass volatile on AudioChain composed in Process, driven from TxAudioIngest's TCI recency / source flag. Or handler swap. Decision to be made in the issue after initial discussion. Do not mutate the operator-visible master bypass state without clear restore semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Automatic source-driven bypass of the TX insert plugin chain when TCI (or designated remote source) is supplying the audio; local mic and WAV playback continue to use the chain; operator master-bypass remains independent or clearly composed; bit-identical pass-through; hot-path safety preserved (no allocs/locks in audio thread); unit tests added; red-light maintainer sign-off obtained before merge; verified on HL2 that behaviour differs correctly by source.

title: [zeus] Multi-tenant TCP relay: per-operator port+secret for remote TCI
description:
Scale the single-user raw-TCP tunnel (zeus-dl6 / zeus-e5e) to a multi-tenant relay so several Zeus operators share one public relay host, each with their OWN remote port AND OWN secret, isolated from each other (operator A's secret cannot open operator B's port / radio).

WHY NOT bore: stock bore has a SINGLE global --secret shared by all clients; any authenticated client can grab any free port. No per-port/per-secret mapping. Running N bore instances per user is impractical — bore's control port is fixed at 7835 and the client always dials it, so you cannot host multiple bore servers behind one IP with distinct control ports.

This is broker territory and converges with the openhpsdrzeus.com broker (zeus-mi5), EXCEPT the relay itself cannot live on that broker: it is a Cloudflare Worker and Workers cannot listen on raw TCP. So the relay must run on a VPS; the web app only manages identities and hands out (port, secret) pairs.
design:
Two viable designs:

OPTION A — frp + small auth web app (off-the-shelf relay, no fork):
frp (fast reverse proxy) supports many frpc clients on one frps, plus a server-plugin system: on each Login/NewProxy registration frps makes an HTTP call to YOUR app to authorize/deny and to pin the remote_port. The small web app holds the operator -> (port, secret) table and answers yes/no. frps does the TCP relay. Standard tunnel-as-a-service pattern. Least code.

OPTION B — native .NET relay (owns both ends, fits Zeus):
Write the relay server in .NET to pair with the native C# tunnel client from zeus-e5e. Client presents (port, secret); server validates against a table (LiteDB/SQL), binds that remote port, proxies bytes. The 'small web app' is the operator-registration panel that issues port+secret. No external daemon; converges with the broker (broker = identity + port/secret issuance on the Worker, relay = VPS .NET service).

Recommendation: Option A if we want something working soon and robust; Option B if this should be an end-to-end Zeus product with full protocol control (reuses the C# client spike).
notes: Context: single-operator self-hosted bore server already validated working on an Ubuntu x86_64 VPS this session (see zeus-e5e notes). This issue is the multi-operator follow-on. Decision A-vs-B still open.

title: [zeus] Spike: native C# bore client (BoreTunnelService) for TCI raw-TCP tunnel
description:
Investigate and prototype a NATIVE C# bore client embedded in Zeus.Server.Hosting (BoreTunnelService) to expose the TCI server (:40001) over a raw-TCP reverse tunnel, instead of bundling the bore binary as a supervised subprocess. This is the recommended approach from zeus-dl6 (the TCI remote-access gap). Living doc — log all bore findings here.

Why native C# over bundling the binary: avoids shipping/signing a third-party executable (no equivalent of zeus-unr macOS notarize / zeus-vo5 Windows Authenticode just for the tunnel), runs anywhere .NET runs, and keeps lifecycle/health/public-URL surfacing fully in-process (no subprocess supervisor / stdout parsing like zeus-b01). Fits Zeus's existing hosted-service pattern.

Open questions to resolve in this spike:
- Pin bore protocol/wire format from source (github.com/ekzhang/bore) and decide version handling.
- Where does the bore SERVER live? bore.pub (free, shared, no-auth, port-collision risk) for dev; self-hosted 'bore server --secret' on a free-tier VPS for prod. NOTE: cannot live on the openhpsdrzeus.com broker — it's a Cloudflare Worker and Workers cannot listen on raw TCP.
- Config surface: server host, control port, requested remote port, secret -> prefs + Server tab.
- Security: HMAC secret on the data path; the public endpoint otherwise lets anyone control the radio.
design:
bore protocol (client side) to reimplement — verify exact wire format against source before coding:
- Connect TCP to server control port (bore.pub default 7835).
- JSON-delimited control messages: Hello(port) to request remote port (0=any, or 40001); if server requires a secret it sends Challenge(uuid) and client replies Authenticate(HMAC-SHA256(secret, uuid)); server confirms assigned port; periodic Heartbeat keepalive.
- Per inbound connection: server sends Connection(uuid); client opens a NEW TCP to the control port, sends Accept(uuid), then bidirectionally proxies bytes between that socket and localhost:40001.
Implementation: ~200-300 lines using TcpClient + System.Text.Json + HMACSHA256, one Task per accepted connection copying streams both ways. Expose current public host:port via typed API for the Server tab. Since TCI control AND audio (RxAudioStream/TxAudioStream binary frames) share the single :40001 WebSocket, the byte-transparent relay carries everything in one connection (see zeus-dl6).
notes:
bore VALIDATED end-to-end this session (2026-06-01) with the stock binary before any C# work.

PUBLIC bore.pub:
- 'bore local 40001 --to bore.pub' -> bore.pub:46264 (random). Plain-TCP connect OPEN; ws:// (no TLS) upgrade returned 101 Switching Protocols (Server: Kestrel) + full TCI handshake (protocol:ExpertSDR3,2.0; device:Zeus; ...).
- 'bore local 40001 --to bore.pub --port 40001' SUCCEEDED -> bore.pub:40001 (fixed port granted because free). Re-verified 101 + handshake. Useful because the TCI client pins port 40001.
- Caveats: bore.pub fixed port is best-effort (fails if held); no auth on the bore.pub data path (anyone with host:port controls the radio).

SELF-HOSTED server VALIDATED (Ubuntu x86_64 VPS):
- Install: github release v0.6.0 x86_64-unknown-linux-musl binary -> /usr/local/bin/bore.
- systemd unit (User=bore system account, EnvironmentFile=/etc/bore.env holding BORE_SECRET from 'openssl rand -hex 32', chmod 600). ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/bore server (dropped --min/--max-port; security = secret + VPS firewall opening only 7835 control + 40001 tunnel). bore reads BORE_SECRET from env.
- Client (Mac): 'bore local 40001 --to VPS_IP --port 40001 --secret <SECRET>'. User confirmed it works correctly.
- LESSON (process): long single-line shell commands wrap in the chat pane and the user's mobile SSH client injects 2 leading spaces at wrap points, corrupting heredocs/base64 on paste. Keep emitted command lines SHORT; split into one-liners.

Client config: WSJT-X/JTDX Rig='Expert Electronics TCI', Network Server='HOST:40001'.

NEXT (multi-tenant): per-operator port+secret tracked in zeus-n99 (stock bore = single global secret only).

title: [zeus] Remote TCI access needs a raw-TCP tunnel — Cloudflare/JWT design can't serve it
description:
GAP: the tunnel epic (zeus-mi5) exposes ONLY port 6060 (web UI + SignalR hub) via Cloudflare Quick Tunnel (cloudflared tunnel --url http://localhost:6060, see zeus-b01), over HTTPS/WSS and gated by Google OAuth + JWT validated on the WebSocket connect (zeus-86w). The TCI server on :40001 is not in scope of any bead (bd search tci = 0 results).

This is structurally incompatible with real TCI clients (WSJT-X/JTDX via Hamlib 'Expert Electronics TCI', Log4OM, N1MM): they only open a plain ws://host:port — no TLS (wss), no OAuth, no JWT, and per the user's constraint cannot run any helper/sidecar on the client machine. cloudflared CANNOT carry raw TCP by design (HTTP/WS only); Tailscale Funnel is likewise TLS-only. So the TCI leg needs a SEPARATE raw-TCP tunnel binary, supervised the same way zeus-b01 supervises cloudflared, pointed at :40001.

Use case: operator runs Zeus+HL2 at home behind a Google Wifi mesh (no router admin, no port-forward) and wants WSJT-X on a remote machine to drive the rig over TCI.
design:
Recommendation: bundle 'bore' (github.com/ekzhang/bore, MIT, single static Rust binary) as a supervised subprocess analogous to zeus-b01, pointed at localhost:40001, requesting a FIXED remote port so the client config is stable. 'bore.pub' is a free public server (no account) but has no auth on the data path — anyone with host:port controls the radio; mitigate with self-hosted 'bore server --secret' on a free-tier VPS (e.g. Oracle Always Free) for a permanent, secret-gated host:port. Alternatives compared: ngrok TCP (works, but fixed address is paid; free = dynamic, forces client reconfig every restart); FRP/SSH -R to a free-tier VPS (stable, free, more setup); Tailscale (stable 100.x IP, raw TCP, free — but requires Tailscale on the CLIENT, which is excluded here).

Key enabler verified in code: TCI audio rides the SAME WebSocket/connection as control — RxAudioStream=1/TxAudioStream=2/IqStream=0 are binary frames with a 64-byte header on :40001 (Zeus.Server.Hosting/Tci/TciStreamPayload.cs). A byte-transparent raw-TCP relay therefore carries control AND audio in one connection; no separate audio path needed. Bandwidth note: RX audio = 48kHz float32 stereo approx 3 Mbit/s upstream; do NOT enable the IQ stream (192kHz) over a remote link.
notes:
TESTS RUN THIS SESSION (2026-06-01, Zeus web mode on :6060, TCI on :40001, HL2 connected):

1. Cloudflare NAMED tunnel (existing ~/.cloudflared/zeus-morrotel.yml: tci.morrotel.eu -> http://localhost:40001) DOES expose TCI as wss://tci.morrotel.eu on 443. Verified: WS upgrade forcing HTTP/1.1 returned '101 Switching Protocols' + full TCI handshake (protocol:ExpertSDR3,2.0; device:Zeus; vfo/modulation/drive...). Over HTTP/2 it returns 400 — a curl artifact (Upgrade header invalid in h2), NOT a tunnel fault. BUT wss is unusable by plain TCI clients, so this does not solve the use case.

2. Firewall ruled out: macOS Application Firewall disabled (socketfilterfw state=0, no block-all, no stealth); no third-party firewall (LuLu/Little Snitch/Murus) process, kext, or system extension. ANOMALY (unresolved, local-only): from the host itself, 127.0.0.1:40001 and the Tailscale IP 100.75.236.17:40001 connect fine, but the en0 LAN IP 192.168.86.21:40001 is CLOSED. Socket is bound IPv6 *:40001 (dual-stack). Not chased further per user; would block any future router port-forward to the LAN IP.

3. ngrok TCP works end-to-end: 'ngrok tcp 40001' -> tcp://2.tcp.eu.ngrok.io:13853 -> localhost:40001. Verified plain-TCP connect OPEN and a plain ws:// (no TLS) upgrade returned '101 Switching Protocols' (Server: Kestrel) + the TCI handshake. Confirms a byte-transparent raw-TCP relay is the correct shape. Rejected by user only for the dynamic address (free tier).

4. playit.gg considered: fixed address is paid. Router port-forward impossible (Google Wifi mesh, no admin password).

title: AI net monitor: transcribe a frequency and report who was on + topics discussed
description:
Leave Zeus tuned to a frequency (e.g. 7.222 MHz) for hours and get back an AI-generated report: a roster of who was on (callsigns, QRZ-enriched names/locations), a topic/timeline summary, and a searchable transcript. An "AI net logger / frequency monitor." Genuinely novel for ham SDR — ASR-on-radio exists in the scanner/trunking world (trunk-recorder + Whisper, OpenMHz) but nobody integrates ASR + callsign extraction + QRZ enrichment + LLM summarization into the SDR itself for HF SSB nets.

Most of the pipeline is straightforward in this stack; ONE part is the real risk (ASR on HF SSB). Plan as two tracks: (1) plumbing, (2) an ASR-quality spike that must pass before committing to track 1.

PIPELINE (and difficulty):
1. Audio capture — EASY. Tap demodulated RX audio, same seam as the CW decoder (zeus CW work / #548) and the proposed SSTV decode.
2. Transmission segmentation — MEDIUM. Detect key-up/key-down / signal-present boundaries; each "over" = one station's turn (better basis for who-spoke than acoustic diarization on a single noisy HF channel).
3. Speech-to-text — HARD (make-or-break). Whisper (whisper.cpp local; M4 Pro runs it well). HF SSB throws QSB/noise/splatter/weak-signal/global-accents/phonetics/Q-codes at it; Whisper hallucinates text on silence/noise, so VAD gating is mandatory. Callsigns are the worst case (not dictionary words).
4. Callsign extraction — MEDIUM. Parse transcript for IDs, decode phonetics ("whiskey one alpha" -> W1A...), then cross-reference QRZ (already integrated) to enrich AND validate the ASR guess against a real callsign.
5. Summarization — EASY/high-value. LLM (Claude API) over the transcript -> topics, participants, timeline, notable items.
6. Report + live view — EASY. Web frontend: building roster, live transcript pane, "generate report" button, searchable archive, reachable from phone.

HONEST EXPECTATION: "surprisingly useful summary with imperfect details," NOT court-transcript accuracy. Plenty for "who was on and roughly what was discussed"; not a verbatim log.

WHY IT FITS ZEUS: audio-tap seam already proven (CW decoder); QRZ integration already exists for enrichment+validation; web/phone frontend for live view and reports; long-running networked backend is exactly right for unattended multi-hour monitoring. Composes with the decode-everything and DXCC-overlay ideas into a situational-awareness layer.

RESPONSIBLE FLAG: amateur transmissions are not private (US Part 97), so personal monitoring/logging is fine. Be thoughtful about PUBLISHING identified transcripts of named individuals — etiquette varies and other countries' rules differ. Default stance: personal-use, think before publishing.

ARCHITECTURE SKETCH: a backend service (Zeus.NetMonitor or similar) or IBackendPlugin+IUiPlugin: RX audio tap -> VAD/transmission segmenter -> per-over buffer -> ASR (whisper.cpp) -> callsign extractor + QRZ enrich -> store segments (LiteDB) -> LLM summarizer on demand/rolling -> SignalR push to frontend.
acceptance_criteria:
PHASE 0 — ASR-quality spike (gate; do this FIRST, before any plumbing):
- Record ~30 min of a real HF SSB net (e.g. 7.222) off the radio.
- Run through whisper.cpp with a ham-context prompt (phonetics, Q-codes, callsign hints).
- Assess: is transcription good enough that callsign extraction + summarization are viable? Document WER/qualitative findings.
- GO/NO-GO decision recorded on this issue before Phase 1.

PHASE 1 — Decode/monitor plumbing (only if Phase 0 is GO):
- RX audio tap feeding a monitor service (pattern on the CW decoder seam).
- Transmission/VAD segmentation produces per-over audio segments.
- ASR transcribes segments; results stored with timestamps.
- Live transcript streams to a frontend panel over SignalR.

PHASE 2 — Enrichment + report:
- Callsign extraction with phonetic decoding; QRZ cross-reference for name/location + validation.
- LLM summarization produces: roster (callsigns+names), topic timeline, notable items.
- Frontend: live roster + transcript, "generate report", searchable archive, phone-accessible.
- Personal-use default; no auto-publish of identified transcripts.
notes: 2026-06-12: Phases 1-3 MERGED to develop (PRs #601/#602/#604) + fix #605; in next nightly. Phase 3 = report/roster/search + per-over audio replay, local-LLM digest (llama-completion child proc, $0/offline), and button-download engine bundles. Engines NOT baked into app (multi-dylib); built static/self-contained per-RID by .github/workflows/build-voyeur-engines.yml (on main via #603/#606), published to release tag voyeur-engines-v1, fetched on demand by in-app setup. osx-arm64 bundles built+published+VERIFIED end-to-end locally (download->extract->run). CI building other 5 RIDs (run 27450402790; win-arm64 failed first pass, iterating). Issue stays OPEN until develop->main release ships. HL2 RX-unaffected bench proof still owed.

title: Design doc: HL2 VNA mode support (docs/designs/)
description:
Write a design document at docs/designs/hl2-vna-mode.md for adding Hermes Lite 2 VNA (vector network analyzer) support to Zeus. Research is DONE (see notes/design fields) — this issue is only to author the design doc; implementation will follow as separate issues once the doc is reviewed by the maintainer (red-light feature: new architecture + new UI).

Scope of the doc: capture the gateware mechanism, exact Protocol-1 register mapping (all already-written registers in Zeus), the FPGA-scan sweep procedure, the measurement/calibration model, the hardware caveats, and the proposed Zeus seams (Protocol1, Server VnaService, Contracts DTOs, frontend Smith chart). Recommend a phased rollout starting with an HL2-verified spike.
design:
RESEARCH COMPLETE — facts for docs/designs/hl2-vna-mode.md.

GATEWARE (softerhardware/Hermes-Lite2, control.v + radio.v):
- VNA enable: reg 0x09, cmd_data[23] = C2 bit 7 (0x80). Confirmed: vna <= cmd_data[23] at cmd_addr 6h09.
- VNA count (N points): reg 0x09, cmd_data[15:0] = C3:C4.
- TX drive: reg 0x09, cmd_data[31:24] = C1 (Zeus already writes here).
- VNA fixed RX gain: reg 0x00 bit 10 (0=-6dB, 1=+6dB).
- Duplex/1RX/48k: reg 0x00 bits [2]/[6:3]/[25:24].
- Start freq: reg 0x01 (Zeus CcRegister.TxFreq, wire 0x02), uint32 Hz.
- Delta Hz/point: reg 0x02 (Zeus CcRegister.RxFreq, wire 0x04), uint32 Hz.
- MOX=1 required (FPGA-scan). TX/RX share NCO -> each output IQ sample = vector ratio at that freq. PA + internal T/R relay disabled in VNA (~vna).
notes:
ALL needed registers ALREADY WRITTEN by Zeus — only frame CONTENT changes, no wire-format/new-register work.

FPGA-SCAN: set count/start/delta; FPGA steps NCO, averages 1024 CORDIC samples/point, emits 1 IQ sample/point, prepends ONE zero IQ sample as block separator. Host re-syncs on the zero. Results arrive as normal RX0 IqFrames (Zeus pipeline already handles).

MEASUREMENT/CAL: M(f)=I+jQ raw vector ratio, needs cal.
- S11/SWR: needs EXTERNAL resistive return-loss bridge + 6-10dB attenuators. OSL cal. SWR=(1+|G|)/(1-|G|), RL=-20log10|G|, Z=50(1+G)/(1-G).
- S21 (filters): TX->DUT->RX + attenuators, thru cal, NO bridge -> good first target.

CAVEATS (load-bearing): measures at low-power ports NOT antenna-through-PA; never TX->RX direct (ADC damage); phase only stable while VNA held -> recal per power cycle, keep VNA on whole session; MUST clear VNA bit on disconnect or it blocks reconnection.

PROPOSED SEAMS:
- Zeus.Protocol1: RadioControlState/CcState add VnaEnabled/VnaPoints/VnaStepHz; ControlFrame.WriteCcBytes set bit10@Config + C2bit7+count@DriveFilter(0x09); Protocol1Client sweep mode diverts IqFrames to VNA consumer.
- Zeus.Server.Hosting: VnaService orchestrates sweep + OSL/thru cal + SWR/RL/Z compute; DTOs in Zeus.Contracts; REST or WS frame.
- Frontend: Smith chart + SWR/RL plot + O/S/L cal flow.

ROLLOUT: doc -> HL2-verified spike (enable VNA + sweep + dump raw N points to log, confirm bit mapping + zero-separator on real hw) -> S21 -> S11 w/OSL -> UI.

SOURCES: HL2 Protocol wiki; HL1 Protocol-Coverage; Quisk SetVNA() (IW0HDV/quisk hermes/quisk_hardware.py); N2ADR VNA reflection-mode google group; EA4GPZ destevez.net mains-choke VNA post.

RED-LIGHT: new architecture + UI -> doc reviewed by maintainer (Brian EI6LF) before impl. Bit mapping bench-verified on HL2 before UI.
close_reason: Design doc written at docs/designs/hl2-vna-mode.md, PR #574 against develop

Unified panel layout (operator's design intent — visual treatment is RED-LIGHT, needs Brian/EI6LF sign-off):
1. SETTINGS/SLIDERS region: keep existing keyer controls (WPM, keyer mode STR/IAM-A/IAM-B, sidetone pitch/gain, macros) — these already round-trip via /api/cw/settings.
2. TERMINAL region: an upper window that shows decoded RX text (from 0x31), and a SINGLE free-text input line at the bottom that the operator types into and transmits (wire to existing /api/cw/send). One terminal, decode-up / type-and-send-down.
3. THRESHOLD SCOPE region: a small audio/envelope view that plots the RX CW envelope with a horizontal THRESHOLD line the operator drags with the mouse to sit just above the noise floor — so noise stops jittering the WPM estimate. Requires backend work: CwAudioDecoder already computes _env/_noiseFloor/_signal and Schmitt hi/lo internally (CwAudioDecoder.cs ~149-171) but does NOT expose them. Need (a) a telemetry path (envelope samples + current noiseFloor/signal) to the frontend, and (b) a way to set a MANUAL threshold that overrides the adaptive Schmitt trigger.

RED-LIGHT items (all need maintainer sign-off before merge, per CLAUDE.md):
- Visual design / layout / unification of the two panels (UX behavior change).
- Wire-format: new envelope-telemetry frame (propose MsgType 0x32) + manual-threshold field/endpoint. Prefer extending /api/cw/settings + a new telemetry frame over reshaping 0x31.
- Panel registration change: collapsing two registered panels ('cw' + 'cwdecoder') into one affects saved layouts — migration/back-compat needs a decision.

Approach: use the frontend-design skill to produce the unified-panel design first (mockup + component plan), get maintainer sign-off, THEN implement backend telemetry + frontend. Build incrementally; do not touch keyer timing, sidetone generation, or TX IQ path.

Supersedes the earlier narrow scope (decode our own TX keying), which folds into the unified terminal's TX line.
design: Reuse Morse FSM from CwAudioDecoder (factor out from the envelope detector — MorseDecode dict + FSM live at CwAudioDecoder.cs:71/247). Source = keyer key-on/off events from CwEngine.cs / CwSidetoneSource.cs (raw-key path), NOT audio. Extend CwDecodedTextFrame (0x31) with an origin/direction flag rather than a new MsgType. Files: Zeus.Server.Hosting/CwEngine.cs, CwSidetoneSource.cs, CwAudioDecoder.cs, CwDecoderService.cs; Zeus.Contracts/CwDecodedTextFrame.cs; zeus-web/src/state/cw-decoder-store.ts, components/design/CwDecoder.tsx, layout/panels/CwDecoderPanel.tsx.
acceptance_criteria: One CW panel replaces both 'cw' and 'cwdecoder'. It exposes the existing keyer settings (WPM, keyer mode, sidetone, macros), a terminal with RX decode above and a free-text TX input line below (wired to /api/cw/send), and an envelope scope with a mouse-draggable threshold line that, when set, pins the decoder's key-detection threshold (stopping noise-driven WPM jitter). Adaptive mode still works when no manual threshold is set. Saved layouts referencing the old panel ids migrate gracefully. All visual/UX/wire-format changes are called out for maintainer sign-off in the PR. Headless-safe; dark+light themes both correct; tokens.css colors only (no new hardcoded hex).

title: [mobile] QR scanner + /go/<slug> redirect handling
description: Mobile scans the QR on Zeus desktop, extracts the openhpsdrzeus.com/go/<slug> URL, and follows the broker's 302 redirect to the current trycloudflare URL while presenting its sealed-cookie session. Mobile remembers the slug for subsequent launches so the user does not re-scan. Handle 'desktop offline' (broker returns a no-tunnel response) gracefully — show 'your shack is offline' rather than a broken redirect.

title: [mobile] Google OAuth login on first install (broker session)
description: Per ADR 0003, mobile pairing relies on the same Google account being signed in on both Zeus desktop and the mobile app. zeus-mobile (Capacitor) needs a first-run flow that authenticates the user against openhpsdrzeus.com's existing OAuth endpoints (66b2c13) and stores the sealed-cookie session locally. Subsequent app launches reuse the session without re-prompting.

title: [zeus] Bundle cloudflared.exe in Windows installer with Authenticode
description: Per ADR 0004, the Windows installer must include cloudflared.exe Authenticode-signed alongside Zeus's own binaries. Update release.yml to download a pinned cloudflared version at build time, sign it, and include it in the MSI/EXE installer payload. Document the installer-size impact (~30 MB).

title: [zeus] Bundle cloudflared in macOS .app and notarize
description: Per ADR 0004, the macOS bundle must include cloudflared inside Zeus.app/Contents/Resources/ (or MacOS/), code-signed and notarized as part of the existing Apple flow. Cloudflare ships unsigned tar.gz releases — the Zeus build pipeline downloads a pinned version at build time, re-signs, and notarizes with the rest of the bundle. Update create-macos-app.sh and zeus-sign-notarize.sh to learn about the embedded binary.

title: [zeus] Server tab: Go Online toggle + QR rendering
description: Extends the Cloudflare Tunnel opt-in card landed in ce1c37e on feature/tunnel. Render a Go Online toggle that spawns cloudflared, displays the stable openhpsdrzeus.com/go/<slug> URL, and shows a scannable QR code for that URL. Surface live status (registering / online / heartbeat-failing / offline). No raw trycloudflare.com URL in the UI — the broker indirection is the operator-facing contract.

title: [zeus] JWKS validator + JWT auth on WebSocket connect
description: Per ADR 0002, every WebSocket connect to Zeus from a paired mobile must present a broker-minted Ed25519 JWT in the Authorization header. Zeus fetches the broker's JWKS endpoint at startup and caches the verifying public key; validates each incoming JWT signature, expiry, audience (this slug), and rejects on failure. JWKS fetch retried on startup if broker unreachable but does not block local desktop operation.

title: [zeus] Heartbeat client to broker /api/register
description: Per ADR 0002, Zeus desktop POSTs {slug, ephemeral_url, secret} to the broker on every cloudflared URL change and every ~60s thereafter. Slug + secret are persisted in zeus-prefs.db (LiteDB) on first sign-in. Retry with exponential backoff on transient broker failure; do not block desktop functionality if broker is down — log + retry.

title: [zeus] Spawn cloudflared subprocess and capture trycloudflare URL
description: Per ADR 0001/0004, Zeus desktop spawns the bundled cloudflared as a subprocess running 'cloudflared tunnel --url http://localhost:6060' and parses stdout for the assigned *.trycloudflare.com URL. Implement a supervisor service that owns the subprocess lifecycle (start, stop, restart on crash), exposes the current URL via a typed API, and surfaces health to the rest of the server. Subprocess must not survive Zeus exit.

title: [broker] Reshape /api/punch to register quick-tunnel URL
description: Per ADR 0001/0002, Zeus desktop now creates its own anonymous Cloudflare Quick Tunnel and reports the random *.trycloudflare.com URL up to the broker. The existing punch handler in https://github.com/brianbruff/openhpsdrzeus.com PR #1 calls the Cloudflare Tunnel API to create a named tunnel under the maintainer CF account. That whole code path becomes dead. Reshape /api/punch to accept {slug, ephemeral_url, secret} from the desktop, persist to D1, and drop the e57294f CF Tunnel API client + DNS provisioning paths inside 77be37b. mine/heartbeat/delete handlers mostly stay; just remove the API-side calls.

title: Unify meter ballistics: shared useBallisticReading hook with peak-hold on by default
description:
The meters in the Meters panel (BigArc/VuColumn/PullDownArc/HBarMeter via MeterRenderer), TxStageMeters (MIC/ALC/PWR/SWR), and MicMeter feel jerky because they update only when the Zustand store ticks (~10 Hz wire frames). useEmaSmoothed runs during render so there's no inter-frame interpolation — the operator sees 10 discrete positions per second.

The AnalogMeter S-meter dial feels right because it has four physics layers: averager prefilter → asymmetric attack/decay RC ballistic → peak-hold ghost → rAF loop publishing at 30 Hz reading the store via getState(). Goal: bake those four layers into one shared hook every meter widget uses.
design:
Per-widget useBallisticReading(id) hook owns its own rAF loop (IntersectionObserver + document.hidden gated), runs averager → attack/decay → peak in VALUE space (not normalized — these widgets have fixed axes), publishes at 30 Hz with 0.001·span threshold, sentinel-passthrough (≤ -200 dBFS bypasses + resets state). Returns { value, peak }.

Universal defaults verbatim from AnalogMeter: attack 50 ms, decay 600 ms, avg 6 samples, peak decay 5 %/s of axis span, peak-hold ON. No per-meter UI; AnalogMeter keeps its existing gear flyout as the power-user surface.

Move the pure ballistic+averager primitives out of analog-meter/analogMeterShared.ts to components/meters/ballistics.ts so both AnalogMeter and the new hook share one implementation (no drift). AnalogMeter imports from new location.

Architecture trade-off: chose per-widget rAF over a central ticker for locality + free visibility gating (no ref-counting needed). N rAF loops is acceptable for the 6-12 visible meters typical.
acceptance_criteria:
- New useBallisticReading hook in zeus-web/src/components/meters/
- Shared ballistics primitives in components/meters/ballistics.ts (moved from analogMeterShared)
- MeterRenderer drops useEmaSmoothed, calls useBallisticReading; HBarMeter receives REAL peak (not peak={value})
- TxStageMeters' four rows drop useEmaSmoothed
- MicMeter drops hand-rolled 1500 ms peak loop
- util/useEmaSmoothed.ts deleted (no remaining callers)
- AnalogMeter unchanged in behaviour (still uses same constants, now from shared module)
- SMeterLive/SMeterDemo NOT touched in this round
- Tests: pure ballistic + averager + peak-decay functions have unit tests
- npm run build green; npm test green
- Bench: meter motion smooth on 28400 MOX TUN (HL2) — no visible 10 Hz stepping
close_reason: PR #550 opened against develop; bench-tested on HL2 with attack=350 ms

Dark theme appearance is unchanged. Colors come from tokens.css theme variables, not hardcoded hex.

Root cause: .cw-stream-hero / .cw-wpm-readout / .cw-macro-edit used --bg-inset/--bg-meter (pinned dark in light theme). Swapped to --bg-0 (one step darker than panel base, flips with theme). Single-file: zeus-web/src/styles/layout.css. Bench-verified light theme on fresh desktop build. PENDING: maintainer (EI6LF) visual sign-off before merge — red-light per CLAUDE.md.

title: Hardware-key sidetone follows ptt_resp (continuous) not cw_key_status (per-element)
description:
Follow-up to zeus-5ue. When keying the HL2 with a paddle, Zeus's CW sidetone stays continuously on instead of following the individual dits/dahs. Root cause: Zeus drives the hardware-key sidetone from C0[0] = ptt_resp (cw_on | ext_ptt), which is level-held HIGH for the whole keyed period including inter-element gaps and CW hang time. It must instead read C0[2] = cw_key_status — the gateware's shaped keyer OUTPUT that toggles per element.

Confirmed with the HL2 gateware author. Response C0 status format (type 2'b00 slot): {3'b000, resp_addr[1:0], cw_key_status, 1'b0, ptt_resp}. So C0[0]=ptt_resp (iresp[30]), C0[2]=cw_key_status (iresp[32]). Upstream doc already notes 'C0[2] follows the signal at the tip, and a shaped CW signal is generated' — Zeus just never wired C0[2].

Today: PacketParser.ExtractHardwarePtt reads (c0Frame0|c0Frame1)&0x01 = C0[0]; that bit -> Protocol1Client.UpdateHardwarePtt -> HardwarePttChanged -> ExternalPttService -> CwSidetoneSource.Down/Up. One Down at key-on, one Up at key-off -> continuous tone.

Fix: add PacketParser.ExtractCwKeyDown reading C0[2] (mask 0x04); add Protocol1Client.CwKeyDownChanged event (mirror HardwarePtt); move the sidetone Down/Up in ExternalPttService onto CwKeyDownChanged (gated to CW mode), keeping HardwarePttChanged (C0[0]) for the MOX takeover path (MOX must stay held through hang time). Packet-rate sampling (~2.6ms at 48k) is ample for 20-40 WPM dit envelopes; CwSidetoneSource's own 5ms raised-cosine smooths edges.

Acceptance: paddle-keyed CW produces sidetone that follows individual dits/dahs (not a continuous tone), with MOX still held across inter-element gaps + hang time.
notes: PR #523 (branch feat/cw-sidetone-cwkey-bit). Sidetone now follows C0[2] cw_key_status (per dit/dah); MOX stays on C0[0] ptt_resp. Bench-verified on HL2 + paddle. Independent of #517.

title: HL2 internal iambic keyer speed/mode never configured — Zeus omits C&C register 0x0B
description:
User report (EA5IUE, HL2+): the CW panel WPM does not reach the Hermes Lite 2. Confirmed against the HL2 gateware — this is a real implementation gap, not host-keying confusion.

## Gateware truth (local copy /Volumes/FastDisk/Radio/HL2/Hermes-Lite2/gateware)

rtl/cw_openhpsdr.sv:27-43 decodes C&C command address 0x0B into the on-board iambic keyer:
- keyer_speed  = cmd_data[13:8]  -> C3[5:0]  (0-60 WPM)
- keyer_mode   = cmd_data[15:14] -> C3[7:6]  (00 straight/ext/bug, 01 Mode A, 10 Mode B)
- keyer_weight = cmd_data[6:0]   -> C4[6:0]  (33-66)
- keyer_spacing= cmd_data[7]     -> C4[7]
- keyer_reverse= cmd_data[22]    -> C2[6]
Related CW regs: 0x0F cw_ptt_delay (C3[7:0]); 0x10 cw_hang_time ({C1,C2[1:0]}); 0x11 mode_b memory timing. rtl/iambic.v documents 1-60 WPM. rtl/dsopenhpsdr1.sv:390 'only enable CWX when keyer is internal'.

Wire mapping: C0 = (0x0B<<1)|mox = 0x16|mox; C1=cmd_data[31:24], C2=[23:16], C3=[15:8], C4=[7:0].

## What Zeus does today

CcRegister enum (Zeus.Protocol1/ControlFrame.cs:65-171) writes only 0x00,0x02,0x04,0x06,0x08,0x0a,0x12,0x14,0x1c,0x56. There is NO 0x0B. So the HL2's internal iambic keyer runs at its gateware default speed/mode regardless of the panel WPM. An operator keying with a paddle on the HL2 KEY jack gets a fixed/default speed; the WPM slider has no effect on hardware-keyed CW.

(Host-side keying via CwEngine -> MorseEncoder.Encode(text,wpm) -> TX IQ ring is a separate, working path: macro/cw_msg sends DO key at the panel WPM. The gap is the hardware iambic keyer only.)

## Doc gap
docs/references/protocol-1/hermes-lite2-protocol.md lists 0x0F (CWX enable) and 0x10 (hang time) but OMITS 0x0B (keyer config) entirely — update the memory map from the gateware while fixing.

## Proposed fix
1. Add CcRegister.CwKeyerConfig = 0x0B and a ControlFrame builder packing speed(C3[5:0]) / mode(C3[7:6]) / weight(C4[6:0]) / spacing(C4[7]) / reverse(C2[6]).
2. RadioService: push 0x0B on connect and whenever the operator changes WPM/mode/weight, gated to HL2 (and other boards that decode 0x0B — verify per board; bare HPSDR/ANAN keyer regs may differ).
3. Decide source of truth for keyer params: reuse CwSettingsStore.Wpm for speed; mode/weight/spacing/reverse need new persisted fields + UI (red-light visual — flag for maintainer).
4. Update the HL2 protocol reference doc with the 0x0B layout.

## Open scope question
Keyer mode/weight/spacing/reverse UI is new surface (operator-felt). Minimum viable fix = forward WPM to 0x0B[C3 5:0] on HL2 so paddle speed tracks the panel; mode/weight/etc can follow.

## Acceptance
With a paddle on the HL2 KEY jack in CW mode, changing the panel WPM changes the hardware keyer speed on the air (verified against a stopwatch), and the protocol-1 reference doc documents register 0x0B.
design:
KEY-TYPE SELECTOR IS REQUIRED (not optional follow-up) — reporter EA5IUE.

keyer_mode = C3[7:6] in register 0x0B (gateware cw_openhpsdr.sv:32):
  00 = straight / external / bug  -> operator keys the line directly; keyer_speed is IGNORED, timing is manual.
  01 = Iambic Mode A
  10 = Iambic Mode B              -> gateware generates dits/dahs from the two paddle inputs at keyer_speed.

Risk if we ship without the selector: if Zeus sends an iambic mode (01/10) by default but the operator has a STRAIGHT key, the gateware treats the single key line as a paddle and keying breaks. Firmwares/operators on straight keys MUST be able to select mode 00. So the CW panel needs a key-type control before/with this lands.

Minimum correct UI: a Straight / Iambic-A / Iambic-B selector. WPM (speed) only meaningful when mode != straight — the panel can dim/disable the WPM slider in straight mode to make that obvious. Weight / spacing / paddle-reverse are further iambic-only refinements that can follow.

Persistence: add KeyerMode (+ later Weight/Spacing/Reverse) to CwSettingsStore alongside Wpm. The mode/selector UI is operator-felt + new visual surface -> RED-LIGHT, needs maintainer (Brian) sign-off on placement/appearance.

Default-safe behaviour: until the operator picks, default to mode 00 (straight) so we never mis-key a straight key. Iambic is opt-in.
notes: PR #524 (branch feat/hl2-cw-keyer-0x0b). CcRegister.CwKeyerConfig=0x16 packs speed C3[5:0] + mode C3[7:6]; RadioService forwards from CwSettingsStore on connect+change via non-MOX round-robin phase 4. UI STR/IAM-A/IAM-B selector, default Straight. Coordinate merge with #517 (both touch layout.css CW section). UI red-light -> Brian.

title: Tunnel Stage 1 DONE: Server tab UI shipped on feature/tunnel
description: Stage 1 UI completed in PR Kb2uka/openhpsdr-zeus#514. https/6443 hint, opt-in checkbox (zeus.tunnel.optIn localStorage), Punch Tunnel button stub. 229/229 zeus-web tests pass. Closing immediately as completed.
close_reason: Stage 1 UI shipped on feature/tunnel, draft PR Kb2uka/openhpsdr-zeus#514

title: Tunnel Stage 4: TunnelAuthMiddleware in Zeus.Server.Hosting
description: Middleware in Zeus.Server.Hosting that detects requests arriving via the tunnel hostname (Host header matches slug stored in LiteDB). LAN/loopback hosts: skip (preserves today's UX). Tunnel host: accept ?t=<jwt> on first hit, verify against broker JWKS (iss=https://openhpsdrzeus.com, aud=<slug>, exp valid, jti not in 10-min replay LRU), set zeus_session cookie (random session id keyed in-process to (sub,email,exp+8h)) scoped to <slug>.openhpsdrzeus.com, 302 to same URL with ?t stripped. Else require valid zeus_session cookie or 401 with HTML pointing back to openhpsdrzeus.com. See spec §Stage 4. Depends on Stage 3.

title: Tunnel Stage 3: TunnelService — cloudflared subprocess management
description: Backend service: on first punch, download platform cloudflared binary from GitHub releases to <app-data>/cloudflared/<version>/, SHA-256 verify. Call broker POST /api/tunnels/punch with stored bearer, receive {slug, hostname, cloudflared_token, jwks_url}, persist to LiteDB. Spawn 'cloudflared tunnel run --token <T>' as child process, capture stderr, expose status via GET /api/broker/tunnel/status. 5-min heartbeat ticker to broker /api/tunnels/heartbeat. Kill subprocess on app exit; on startup adopt living PID or kill stale. See spec §Stage 3. Depends on Stage 2.

title: Tunnel Stage 2: BrokerAuthController — Google sign-in in Zeus
description:
Zeus desktop authenticates to the openhpsdrzeus.com broker via Google OAuth on first run, claims a random slug, and stores the bearer token + slug in LiteDB (zeus-prefs.db) for subsequent sessions. Flow: user clicks Go Online → system browser opens broker's /api/auth/google/start → user signs in → broker callback redirects to loopback URL Zeus is listening on → Zeus extracts bearer token + assigned slug → persists. Subsequent /api/register and /api/heartbeat calls use the stored bearer.

This is the desktop-side equivalent of zeus-m06 (mobile). The bearer token replaces what Stage 2 of the original tunnel design called BrokerAuthController.

title: Broker deploy: provision openhpsdrzeus.com Worker
description:
One-time setup to bring the broker online. Runbook in brianbruff/openhpsdrzeus.com README.

Post-pivot (ADR 0001/0002): the broker no longer provisions Cloudflare named tunnels under the maintainer account, so the CF API secrets are no longer needed. New secret list:

Steps:
- wrangler d1 create openhpsdrzeus-broker
- wrangler kv namespace create KV
- fill database_id + id into wrangler.toml
- set secrets: SESSION_SECRET, GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET
- create OAuth client in Google Cloud Console (redirect URI https://openhpsdrzeus.com/api/auth/google/callback)
- wrangler d1 migrations apply --remote
- wrangler deploy

Smoke check: curl /healthz returns ok; curl /.well-known/jwks.json returns Ed25519 JWK.

Removed from original list (no longer needed under Quick Tunnel architecture): CF_API_TOKEN, CF_ACCOUNT_ID, CF_ZONE_ID.

title: Tunnel epic: Cloudflare tunnel for remote radio access
description:
Expose each Zeus.Server to a paired mobile client over the public internet via anonymous Cloudflare Quick Tunnels, gated by a same-Google-account binding through the openhpsdrzeus.com broker. Architecture pinned in ADRs 0001-0004 on this branch (feature/tunnel, docs/adr/).

Pivot from original design: the broker no longer provisions named tunnels under the maintainer's Cloudflare account. Zeus desktop spawns its own anonymous cloudflared Quick Tunnel; broker only holds Google identity, mints Ed25519 redirect JWTs, and serves /go/<slug> as a 302 to the current ephemeral *.trycloudflare.com URL.

Components:
- Broker (separate repo brianbruff/openhpsdrzeus.com, branch feature/tunnel-broker) — ~70%% of existing 16-commit work survives. Reshape covered by zeus-wdg.
- Zeus desktop — bundled cloudflared, subprocess supervisor, heartbeat, JWKS-validated JWT auth, Go Online UI.
- Mobile (zeus-mobile) — Google OAuth on first install, QR scanner, redirect handling.

Sub-issues:
  zeus-6a4  Broker deploy: provision openhpsdrzeus.com Worker
  zeus-ce3  Zeus desktop Google sign-in to broker (claim slug, store bearer)
  zeus-wdg  [broker] Reshape /api/punch to register quick-tunnel URL

  zeus-bxl  [zeus]   Heartbeat client to broker /api/register
