title: P1: Implement onboard codec mic-in (radio mic → WDSP TXA) — Phase B of #426
description:
Phase A landed in commit fix(p1) for #426 — RX audio now reaches the radio's headphone jack on Hermes / ANAN-class / OrionMkII boards via EP2 L/R bytes.

Phase B is the other direction: route the EP6 mic samples (off+6, off+7 in PacketParser.cs) into WDSP TXA so operators can plug a mic into the radio's front-panel jack and have it drive TX (the way Thetis / linHPSDR / piHPSDR work on Hermes).

Design considerations:
- TxAudioIngest already accepts source-tagged mic blocks (TCI vs browser/native). Add a third tagged source 'RadioCodec' so the existing block-handling, MOX-edge ring-drain, and accumulator path are reused.
- Sample-rate handling: P1 EP6 mic samples come at the RX sample rate. WDSP TXA wants 48 kHz blocks. At RX=48 kHz it's 1:1; at higher RX rates the radio repeats the mic sample N times. Decimate (take 1-in-N) at the parse seam, or skip the feature when RX rate != 48 kHz for MVP.
- LrAudioSwap: Hermes-class boards have LrAudioSwap=true per BoardCapabilities — does this affect inbound mic too, or is that only outbound L/R? Verify against Thetis source before assuming symmetry.
- Operator UI: source-selection toggle (radio mic / browser mic / TCI). Default for boards with HasOnboardCodec=true is radio mic (matches Thetis). Default for HL2 is browser mic (today's behaviour).

Acceptance:
- On Hermes with a mic in the front-panel jack: SSB / FM transmitted audio matches Thetis behaviour.
- On HL2: unchanged (browser mic still drives TXA).
- Operator can toggle source from UI.
- Bench test on Hermes hardware required (Brian has only HL2).

title: Logbook displays QSO time in local time instead of UTC
description:
The logbook UI renders QSO timestamps in the browser's local timezone, but ham-radio convention is to ALWAYS show QSO times in UTC. The operator reported this thinking it also affected the QRZ.com upload — investigation shows that only the visual rendering is wrong; the wire/persistence path is already UTC throughout:

Wire/storage path (already correct, no changes needed):
- POST/GET /api/log/entries carries qsoDateTimeUtc as ISO-8601 UTC (Zeus.Server.Hosting/LogService.cs:82 stores DateTime.UtcNow when the client omits it).
- ADIF export at LogService.cs:213-214 emits QSO_DATE / TIME_ON from QsoDateTimeUtc directly (no timezone conversion).
- QRZ.com publish at Zeus.Server.Hosting/QrzService.cs:408-409 emits QSO_DATE / TIME_ON from entry.QsoDateTimeUtc directly. Verified: QRZ.com receives correct UTC.

UI bug (fix needed):
- zeus-web/src/components/design/LogbookLive.tsx:68-75 uses date.toLocaleTimeString() and date.toLocaleDateString() with no timezone option, which converts the UTC source to the browser's local timezone. An operator in CET sees a QSO logged at 19:30Z rendered as '21:30' on the wall clock.

Fix: pass { timeZone: 'UTC' } to both toLocale*String calls, and append a 'Z' or 'UTC' suffix on the rendered string so the operator can see at a glance that the column is UTC and won't second-guess it. Same fix wherever LogEntry.qsoDateTimeUtc is rendered (grep for qsoDateTimeUtc in zeus-web/src to catch any other surfaces — at minimum LogbookLive but possibly the logbook export filename and any inspector views).

Acceptance: a QSO logged while the browser is in a non-UTC timezone shows as the UTC clock time in the logbook column, and the column header / cell tooltip makes it clear the time is UTC. ADIF export and QRZ upload remain unchanged (already correct).
close_reason: Fixed in develop via PR #488 (commit 4d5664f) — forces UTC on logbook display + wire

title: CW carrier appears wider than expected on the panadapter
description:
Bench observation 2026-05-24 (after zeus-o27 visual validation): the host-side CW carrier renders as a noticeably wide signal on the HL2 panadapter — wider than a clean Morse keying envelope should produce. Expected width for a 5 ms raised-cosine ramp at 600 Hz pitch is roughly ±100 Hz of skirt; the observed signal looks closer to 1–2 kHz wide.

Possible causes to investigate (in order of likelihood):
- Ramp time too short → key clicks. Bump RampMs from 5 to 8–10 in CwEngine.cs and re-measure.
- Spectral artefacts from the integer-truncated phase accumulator at large basebandHz values (under CTUN the baseband can be ±9 kHz; phase wraps less often relative to the carrier).
- Sample-rate / interpolation interaction with the HL2 CIC chain that's specific to the 0x02 EP2 path.
- Panadapter averaging / bin width making any signal look wider than it is — sanity-check by comparing against a known-good Thetis transmission on the same band/zoom.

Acceptance: photograph the carrier under (a) current code (5 ms ramp) and (b) bumped ramp (8 ms), compare. If still wide, add a server-side spectral probe at the TX IQ ring exit to confirm the IQ matches the expected envelope spectrum.
close_reason: Closed at maintainer request (rampa069) — not pursuing the wide-CW-carrier investigation.

title: CW engine + /api/cw/send (PR 1 of 4)
description:
First PR for [[zeus-4np]]. Get a single text-to-CW round-trip working end-to-end so the wire path is validated before we touch UI / TCI / sidetone.

## Scope

- Add Zeus.Server.Hosting/CwEngine.cs (hosted singleton). API:
  - Task SendAsync(string text, int wpm, CancellationToken)
  - Task StopAsync()  // graceful drain
  - Task AbortAsync() // hard stop
  - event Action<CwEngineStatus> Status
- Backend implementation: P/Invoke to WDSP CWX (CWXSetKeyerSpeed, CWXSetCharSpeed, CWXSetMode, CWXToBuf). Confirm WDSP CWX support is actually available in our build before committing — if not, fall back to a hand-rolled cosine-squared envelope into TxIqRing (still small).
- TxService.TrySetMox needs a MoxSource enum (UI=default / TCI / Hardware / Cwx). Hardware path from [[zeus-xcz]] gets tagged 'Hardware', the new engine claims 'Cwx'. Release rule: only the owning source can drop MOX (UI override always wins; trip always wins).
- POST /api/cw/send { text, wpm? } → engine.SendAsync. Returns 202 immediately, status streamed via hub.
- POST /api/cw/abort → engine.AbortAsync.
- Unit tests:
  - Encoder: 'CQ DE EI6LF' produces correct dit/dah sequence with correct timings at 20 wpm.
  - MoxSource: external falling edge with Source=Hardware doesn't release MOX if current source is Cwx (and vice versa).
- Bench test: curl POST /api/cw/send 'CQ' against the HL2 → carrier on the air for the right duration.

## Out of scope (later PRs in epic)

- UI wiring (PR 2).
- Macros store, queue display (PR 2).
- TCI hookup (PR 3).
- Sidetone monitor routing (PR 4).
acceptance_criteria: curl -X POST /api/cw/send -d '{"text":"CQ","wpm":20}' produces a 4-element CW transmission at 20 wpm on the HL2 against the current VFO/mode (CWU/CWL). MOX UI follows. dotnet test passes (new encoder + MoxSource tests). No regressions in [[zeus-xcz]].
close_reason: PR #477 merged into develop as commit 9016f29 (squash) on 2026-05-24 13:05Z, approved by Kb2uka. End-to-end CW keying validated on live HL2.

title: CW keyer: end-to-end host-side keying (epic)
description:
Make the existing CW Keyer panel (zeus-web/src/components/design/CwKeyer.tsx + layout/panels/CwPanel.tsx) actually transmit. Today it is a mockup: WPM slider only writes to a React useState, the 6 macro buttons take an onSend prop the panel never passes, the morse stream is hardcoded text, no /api/cw endpoint exists, TciSession.cs:595 logs 'no CW engine yet' for cw_macros/cw_msg/keyer. The only working CW path is the hardware key plugged into the HL2's rear KEY jack (followed via [[zeus-xcz]]).

## Use cases in scope

1. **Macros from the UI** — click 'CQ CQ CQ' → real RF on the air.
2. **TCI keyer** — MSHV / N1MM-like clients POST cw_msg via TCI and the engine transmits.
3. **Sidetone monitor** — when CW transmits (engine or hardware key), Zeus plays sidetone in the host audio out, not just on the HL2's headphone jack.

## Architectural shape

- **CW engine** (Zeus.Server.Hosting / new file): owns a queue of (char, dit-ms, dah-ms, intra/inter-spacing) events. Two backends to consider — preferred: P/Invoke into WDSP's CWX (CWXSetKeyerSpeed / CWXSetMode / CWXToBuf — already loaded for us); fallback: handcrafted cosine-squared envelope into TxIqRing.
- **MOX source tracking** — TxService.TrySetMox needs a  enum (UI / TCI / Hardware / Cwx) so the engine can claim/release without fighting [[zeus-xcz]] or UI button. Extend the contract introduced when fixing hardware-PTT propagation.
- **/api/cw endpoints** — POST /api/cw/send {text}, POST /api/cw/stop, POST /api/cw/abort. Status broadcast via existing StreamingHub.
- **CwSettingsStore** — LiteDB store for WPM, Farnsworth (optional), macros (6 default slots, editable), sidetone gain/pitch.
- **UI rewire** — CwPanel passes onSend that POSTs /api/cw/send. Macros editable inline + persisted. Queue display shows characters as they leave. STOP button calls /api/cw/abort. Sidetone gain slider.
- **TCI hookup** — replace the four stub cases in TciSession.cs:595-601 with calls into the engine.

## PR plan (incremental, each shippable on its own)

1. zeus-? — engine core + /api/cw/send happy path. WDSP CWX P/Invoke wired through TxIqRing. MOX source enum added in pass.
2. zeus-? — CwSettingsStore + UI rewire (real onSend, editable macros, WPM persisted, queue display, stop).
3. zeus-? — TCI keyer hookup against the engine.
4. zeus-? — sidetone monitor: route WDSP TXA sidetone to RX audio mixer when CWX or hardware key drives MOX.

Open child issues per PR (this epic stays open until all four land).
acceptance_criteria: Click a macro button on the CW panel and the HL2 transmits the timed CW characters at the configured WPM, the MOX UI follows, the queue display shows characters being sent, sidetone is audible in the Zeus audio out, and an MSHV-style TCI client can drive the same engine via cw_msg.
close_reason: All 4 child PRs merged: #477, #479, #504, #508 (+ follow-ups #516, #517, #523, #524). Epic complete.

title: HL2 external CW/PTT key state not propagated to host (Zeus stays unkeyed)
description:
When the operator keys the HL2 via the rear KEY jack (or external PTT line), the HL2 gateware transmits a shaped CW carrier on its own (no host TX IQ required — see docs/references/protocol-1/hermes-lite2-protocol.md:200). The radio echoes the key state on every EP6 USB frame via C0[0]=PTT/MOX and C0[2]=tip-follow, but Zeus discards those bits:

- Zeus.Protocol1.PacketParser.TelemetryReading preserves the C0 byte, but every consumer reads only the address (bits 7:3). TxMetersService even masks C0[0] out explicitly (C0AddrMask = 0x7E) with a comment acknowledging it as 'the PTT/MOX echo'.
- TxService.TrySetMox is only called from /api/mox, TCI sessions, and internal flows — never from inbound packets.
- Net effect with external CW keying: HL2 transmits at full output, but Zeus never sees MOX-on. WDSP TXA stays quiescent, the MOX UI indicator stays off, TX meters stay at idle cadence, PsAutoAttenuateService doesn't gate, the PRD FR-6 120 s timeout isn't armed, and the SWR trip is disarmed.

## Fix shape (root-cause)

1. Decode C0[0] from the inbound packet (already on the wire). C0[2] is redundant on HL2 for our purposes — C0[0] is OR'd with the tip state by the gateware, so just reading C0[0] catches both external PTT and CW key.
2. Track raw bit + rising/falling debounce. Rising edge → immediate MOX-on. Falling edge → MOX-off after a CW-hang window (~250 ms) of continuous low, so inter-element spaces don't drop TX.
3. Plumb through a 'MOX source' tag (UI / TCI / External) on TxService.TrySetMox so an externally-triggered MOX can be released by the same external path *without* a UI-initiated MOX getting cancelled by a transient external low.

## Test plan

- Unit: PacketParser preserves C0[0] (already true) — keep covered.
- Unit: ExternalPtt service rising/falling debounce with a fake clock.
- Bench: external CW paddle on HL2 → MOX indicator follows, TX meters tick at 10 Hz, SWR trip armed, hang holds MOX through inter-character spaces.
- Bench: UI MOX still works and isn't released by transient inbound C0[0]=0 between TX-IQ bursts.
acceptance_criteria: External CW/PTT keying of HL2 raises Zeus MOX (UI indicator on, TX meters at MOX cadence, SWR trip armed, FR-6 timeout running). Releasing the key drops MOX after a configurable hang (~250 ms). UI / TCI MOX paths still work and are not corrupted by inbound echoes.
notes: PR #476 opened against develop: https://github.com/Kb2uka/openhpsdr-zeus/pull/476
close_reason: Merged to develop (PR #476, commit c2178fa)

title: Research: HL2 dual-antenna QRM eliminator feasibility
description:
Spike for [[zeus-s0e]]. Decide whether a Zeus QRM eliminator using two antennas on (or around) the Hermes-Lite 2 is feasible, and if so, which architecture wins.

## Investigation areas

1. **HL2 hardware survey**
   - Stock HL2 v2: confirm there is exactly one RF SMA + one ADC. Are there published mods that add a second ADC path or a high-impedance noise-antenna front end? Note product names (HL2 v3 if it exists, N7DDC variants, etc.) and any community schematics.
   - The 'openHPSDR Rx Antenna' bit in the HPSDR protocol (see docs/references/protocol-1/hermes-lite2-protocol.md:153–157) is a switch, not a parallel input — confirm and document that two antennas cannot be sampled simultaneously on stock HL2.

2. **Dual-HL2 setup**
   - Can two HL2 boards share a clock / PPS reference well enough to give two coherent IQ streams that the host can phase-align? Check the HL2 sync header / community designs (mi0bot, jeremymcdermond fork, etc.).
   - What would the host plumbing look like? Today Zeus.Protocol1.Discovery and Protocol1Client assume one radio per session — would we need a second client instance and a software combiner?

3. **External analog canceller + Zeus plugin**
   - Model: MFJ-1026 / X-Phase / DXEngineering NCC-1 sit between two antennas and the radio's single RX input. Zeus would just expose a control plugin (cf. existing 4O3A plugins in the sibling openhpsdr-zeus-plugins registry — Antenna Genius, PGXL, TGXL).
   - List candidate devices that have a documented control protocol (some are purely analog/mechanical and not pluginable).

4. **DSP algorithm survey (assuming we get two coherent streams)**
   - Adaptive canceller variants: LMS, NLMS, RLS, frequency-domain Wiener. Which already exist in WDSP? Read docs/lessons/wdsp-init-gotchas.md and Zeus.Dsp/Wdsp/* before assuming we have to write one from scratch.
   - Latency / sample-rate constraints. The canceller has to run at the IQ rate, not the post-WDSP audio rate, otherwise the phase coherence is gone.

5. **Plugin architecture fit**
   - Today IAudioPlugin (docs/plugins/audio-plugins.md) processes audio samples, not IQ. A genuine RF-domain QRM eliminator would need a new extension surface (IRxIqPlugin or similar) — that is a core architecture change, red-light per CLAUDE.md.
   - Verdict for each architecture path: 'plugin-only', 'plugin + small core hook', or 'must live in core'.

## Output

A short design doc under docs/proposals/qrm-eliminator-hl2.md (one or two pages) that:
- Confirms or refutes the single-HL2 software path.
- Picks one of the three remaining paths (dual-HL2, external canceller plugin, drop the project) as the recommendation.
- Lists the implementation work the recommended path implies, and which of it can live in a plugin vs. needs a core change.

When the doc lands and is reviewed, close this and refine [[zeus-s0e]] into concrete implementation issues — or close [[zeus-s0e]] with a 'not feasible' note.
acceptance_criteria: docs/proposals/qrm-eliminator-hl2.md exists with a clear recommendation. [[zeus-s0e]] is either refined into implementation issues or closed with rationale.
close_reason:
Single-HL2 software path ruled out: HL2 has one ADC (RxAdcCount=1, BoardCapabilitiesTable.cs) and the 'openHPSDR Rx Antenna' protocol bit is a switch, not a parallel input — there is no way to get two simultaneous IQ streams from one HL2. True QRM cancellation needs spatial diversity (≥2 antennas + ≥2 synchronous ADCs); without it the problem is mathematically subdetermined.

Single-antenna noise tools (WDSP NR/NR2/ANF/NB/SNB) are an unrelated project, not a QRM eliminator.

Remaining HW-dependent paths (two HL2 clock-sync, external analog canceller + plugin, HL2 mod with 2nd ADC) are deferred — open a new issue if any of them becomes a realistic target.

title: Refresh wiki, docs, and READMEs to current app state
description:
The wiki (https://github.com/Kb2uka/openhpsdr-zeus/wiki), in-repo docs/, and README.md have drifted behind the current state of Openhpsdr-Zeus. Audit and update so a new user / contributor sees an accurate picture.

Scope:
- README.md — radio status lines + high-level feature list (keep tight; per CLAUDE.md the README is intentionally short)
- GitHub wiki — extensive feature write-ups, per-panel guides, how-tos
- docs/ — lessons, RCAs, references, design plans that may reference removed/renamed seams

Cover at minimum:
- Single-binary OpenhpsdrZeus host + --desktop flag (Photino) vs web mode (Vite + :6060)
- Native miniaudio RX/TX path (post audio-native rollup 2026-05-16)
- HpsdrBoardKind unification (P1+P2), OrionMkIIVariant, BoardCapabilitiesTable, RadioDriveProfile, PaDefaults, RadioCalibrations seams
- HL2 PureSignal panel (auto-attenuate, auto-cal, hw_peak guidance)
- LiteDB as single source of truth (rotator collapse, TX filter + last-freq persistence)
- Plugin system rebuild + registry repo kb2uka/openhpsdr-zeus-plugins
- TCI server (single instance) status
- Multi-panadapter status (#155 Phase 1 on claude/support-multiple-panadapters)
- Panfall drag behaviour change (#470)
- Current radio support matrix (Metis/Hermes/HermesII/ANAN-10/10E/100/100B/100D/200D/OrionMkII family/HermesC10/HL2)

Acceptance:
- README radio status table reflects what actually works today
- Wiki has at least: Getting Started, Panels overview, PureSignal (HL2), Plugin system, Troubleshooting
- docs/ has no stale references to removed code paths (grep for old type names; spot-check lessons)
notes: Docs effort should be structured so the Chrome Claude extension can capture rich screenshots of the running app for the wiki and per-panel guides — i.e. include explicit 'open this URL / route / panel' steps that an agent driving Chrome can follow, and embed the resulting screenshots in the wiki pages.

title: Add ?desktop=1 query param to suppress mobile layout swap (#472)
description: Mirror the existing ?mobile=1 override with ?desktop=1 in zeus-web/src/mobile/MobileApp.tsx useIsMobileViewport() so operators can keep their custom desktop layout in a narrow browser window (e.g. a small touchscreen as a secondary control surface). KISS — no settings UI, no prefs key, just the query param. If both ?mobile=1 and ?desktop=1 are set, ?desktop=1 wins. Tracks GitHub issue #472.
close_reason: PR #473 opened against develop; closes GH #472

title: Support macOS Intel x64 (non-Apple Silicon)
description: Add macOS x64 (Intel) support alongside the existing arm64 (Apple Silicon) build. Covers: .NET RID osx-x64 publish target, native WDSP library built for x86_64 (or universal binary), miniaudio vendored build for x64, Photino desktop runtime verification, and CI workflow updates to produce an osx-x64 artifact. Validate on an Intel Mac before shipping. Goal: Intel Mac users on macOS can run Openhpsdr-Zeus without Rosetta.
design:
INVESTIGATION (2026-05-30):
Most of the pipeline is already x64-ready:
- OpenhpsdrZeus.csproj: no hardcoded RID (driven by -r at publish). Agnostic.
- installers/create-macos-app.sh: already takes arch arm64|x64, validates, launchers export DYLD for both.
- release.yml: macOS sign/notarize/staple/gatekeeper steps ALREADY branch on x64 via ternary (rid=='osx-arm64' && 'arm64' || 'x64').
- Photino.NET 4.0.16 ships osx-x64 native (present in bin/.../runtimes/osx-x64/native/Photino.Native.dylib).

Missing (mechanical/easy) in release.yml:
- a build-native-macos-x64 job
- matrix entry osx-x64 in build-release
- a case osx-x64 in 'Map RID to native artifact name'
- add to needs:, create-release files:, and release notes.

REAL BLOCKER (not easy): how to build the x86_64 WDSP+FFTW.
- macos-13 Intel runner: only clean path (brew FFTW bottles are arm-only on ARM runners). BUT maintainer DELIBERATELY dropped Intel — release.yml line ~946 release notes say 'Intel Mac builds are no longer published — the macos-13 runner queue ... too slow to be practical.' Re-adding reverts a documented decision. Also GitHub is sunsetting macos-13 Intel runners.
- Cross-compile on ARM runner: blocked by arm-only Homebrew FFTW; would need x86_64 FFTW vendored/built by hand.

VERDICT: red-light — reverses a documented maintainer release decision and cannot be validated without an Intel Mac. Needs Brian/Doug sign-off on whether + how before coding.
notes:
STATUS 2026-05-30 — PARKED. CI work-in-progress on branch feature/macos-x64 (remote tip ce0ec04, NOT merged, no PR). Single commit on top of develop adds the osx-x64 cross-compile job + matrix entry + signed DMG wiring + release/nightly notes.

WHAT WORKS: pkg-config installs (pkgconf 2.5.1), the x86_64 sonoma FFTW bottle downloads + extracts (verified x86_64 via lipo). The LOCAL spike on an arm64 Mac fully proved the cross-build (x86_64 libwdsp, 8 SBNR exports) using PKG_CONFIG_PATH — so the mechanism is sound.

WHAT STILL FAILS (3 dry-run attempts, runs 26680688839 / 26681020945 / 26681954806): the 'Configure CMake (x86_64)' step dies at CMakeLists.txt:153 'FFTW3 not found' on the clean runner, even after:
  (1) installing pkg-config, and
  (2) adding prefix= to the generated .pc files (the diagnose step proved pkgconf 2.5.1 reported 'Package fftw3 not found in pkg-config search path' for prefix-less .pc; adding prefix= did NOT fix it — still 'FFTW3 not found').
So on the GH runner, CMake's pkg_check_modules still isn't resolving our staged x86_64 FFTW .pc even with PKG_CONFIG_PATH set and prefix= present. Root cause not yet pinned (possible: cmake 4.3.2 FindPkgConfig env handling, or pkgconf path-resolution quirk, or the .pc still missing a field pkgconf 2.x requires).

DIAGNOSE OUTPUT captured (run 26681847920): which pkg-config=/opt/homebrew/bin/pkg-config v2.5.1; PKG_CONFIG_PATH correctly set to the pc dir; .pc files present with correct libdir/includedir; yet 'pkg-config --exists fftw3' => FAIL rc=1 'not found in search path'. That was the prefix-less version; the prefix= version (commit ce0ec04) STILL fails configure but I did not re-capture pkg-config --exists for it.

NEXT IDEAS (untried), in rough order of robustness:
  - Bypass pkg-config entirely: extract the x86_64 bottle into /usr/local/opt/fftw and let WDSP CMakeLists' Homebrew fallback (lines ~138-150, which checks /usr/local/opt/fftw for x86_64) find it. No pkg-config dependency at all. PROBABLY THE RIGHT FIX.
  - Or pass -DFFTW3_ROOT / -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH to the staged tree and rely on the find_package(FFTW3 CONFIG) path.
  - Or debug why pkgconf 2.5.1 won't honor PKG_CONFIG_PATH (try PKG_CONFIG_PATH with trailing behavior, or 'pkg-config --debug').

CLEANUP NOTE: branch feature/macos-x64 + 3-4 dispatched dry-run runs exist on GitHub Actions; abandoned. The local arm64 build's committed Zeus.Dsp/runtimes are untouched. NOT validated on real Intel hardware regardless — that remains a hard requirement before any merge.

Decision: PARKED at user request; not worth more session time now.

title: Verify CESSB always-on (no user toggle, KISS)
description: Audit Thetis CESSB (Controlled Envelope SSB) implementation and ensure it is unconditionally active in Zeus. Do NOT expose an enable/disable option in the UI or prefs — CESSB must always be on. Cross-check Thetis source for the WDSP CESSB init/enable calls, mirror them in Zeus TX init, and confirm no code path can disable it at runtime. KISS: no setting, no prefs key, no API.
notes: REVIEW (overnight): approved. Single-file flip (WdspDspEngine.cs) from SetTXAosctrlRun(id,0) to (id,1) at TX channel open + matching init log + comment block citing Thetis dsp.cs:240. No prefs/REST/UI surface — KISS confirmed. Conventional commit prefix, no AI mentions. Build green (0 warn/0 err). Stayed strictly in scope; no defaults touched. Close decision correct.
close_reason: CESSB now unconditionally enabled at TX init; no toggle exists (no prefs, no REST, no UI)

title: Meters panel: min width = one vertical bar, left-align extra bars
description:
Meters panel currently mis-sizes when only a single vertical bar is shown and stretches/centers additional bars.

Fix:
- Minimum width of the meters panel should accommodate exactly one vertical bar (no extra slack).
- When more vertical bars are added, they should be left-aligned within the panel — not stretched to fill available space, not centered.

Affects layout/flex rules in the meters panel container; verify on HL2 in RX and on TX with multiple TX-stage meters visible.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #474

title: Sliders should react live during drag, not just on release
description:
Several Zeus sliders only push their value to the backend when the operator finishes the drag (mouseup / debounce flush). Per Brian: every slider should react live as it moves so the operator hears/sees the change in real time, then commit on release.

Examples observed:
- AF gain slider (zeus-web/src/components/AfGainSlider.tsx) — currently 100 ms debounce + onMouseUp commit.

Acceptance:
- Audit every slider in the frontend (AF, AGC-T, drive/tune, filter-cut, mic gain, leveler, RIT/XIT, etc.).
- Each one streams intermediate values during drag with the lightest possible debounce (or rAF throttle) so the audible/visible response is immediate, then commits the final value on release.
- No regressions: backend isn't flooded (rAF or ~16-33 ms throttle is fine), inflight requests still cancel on supersede.
- Keyboard / touch paths behave the same.

Not in scope: rewriting the request pipeline; this is a per-slider behavior change.
notes: REVIEW (overnight): approved. New hooks/useLiveSlider.ts cleanly owns rAF coalesce + abort-on-supersede + flush; cleanup on unmount; jsdom setTimeout fallback for tests. Nine sliders migrated (AF, AGC, ATT, DRV, TUN, LVLR, MIC, Zoom, MobileZoom) — biggest behaviour wins are AGC-T and S-ATT which previously POSTed only on release. Optimistic store updates preserved per-component (correct call — store shape varies). 6 new unit tests + 213 prior tests all pass. Build green. No defaults / palette / scope changes. Close decision correct.
close_reason: Closed

title: Make CTUN the default/only mode + add click-drag pan on panadapter & waterfall
description:
Two related UX changes to the panadapter/waterfall tuning model.

## 1. Remove the CTUN toggle

CTUN (issue #427, commit 893b94e) is the desired tuning behavior — dial roams, hardware NCO stays put. Make it the default and only mode. The legacy "clicks retune the radio" path goes away.

Concrete work:
- Drop the CTUN button from the bottom transport bar in `zeus-web/src/App.tsx` (around lines 775-793).
- Remove the `ctunEnabled` toggle plumbing from `connection-store.ts`, `api/client.ts`, and `ZeusEndpoints.cs` (POST `/api/radio/ctun` or equivalent).
- In `RadioService.cs` / `DspPipelineService.cs`, collapse the CTUN-on branches to be unconditional; delete the CTUN-off legacy paths.
- `Zeus.Contracts/Dtos.cs:259` — keep `RadioLoHz` (still needed to express the frozen NCO position), drop `CtunEnabled`.
- `RadioStateStore.cs:157` — drop the persisted CTUN flag; `RadioLoHz` still persists. Handle hydration of any existing prefs DBs gracefully (treat missing field as "always on").
- Update inline comments that say "when CTUN is on / off" to reflect the unconditional model.
- Tests in `tests/Zeus.Server.Tests/` that exercise the toggle need updating.

## 2. Click-drag to pan the panadapter/waterfall window

When the operator presses (mouse-down or touch-down) on the panadapter or waterfall and drags horizontally, the visible window should pan left/right — frequencies above (drag left) or below (drag right) the current visible range scroll into view. Release ends the drag.

This is panning the *view window* (the radio NCO / hardware center), not retuning the dial:
- The dial (`VfoHz`) stays where it is on the operator's tuned signal.
- The hardware NCO (`RadioLoHz`) moves to follow the drag, so new spectrum scrolls in.
- The waterfall's existing horizontal-shift path should handle the visual continuity (don't flip axes — see `docs/lessons/dev-conventions.md`).
- Touch and mouse parity. Don't break existing click-to-tune: a click-without-drag still places the dial at the click X. Drag threshold (~5 px) before pan kicks in.
- Inertia/momentum is out of scope for v1 — just direct drag.

Files likely involved: `zeus-web/src/components/Panadapter.tsx`, `Waterfall.tsx`, `PassbandOverlay.tsx`, `gl/panadapter.ts`, and whatever backend endpoint moves `RadioLoHz` independently of `VfoHz` (RadioService.cs around the CTUN toggle path may be the right seam now that #1 removes the toggle).

## Why bundled

Removing the toggle simplifies the drag implementation — there's only one tuning model to reason about. Do #1 first, then layer #2 on the simpler code.

## Acceptance criteria
- CTUN button no longer appears in the UI.
- No `CtunEnabled` field on the wire, no toggle endpoint.
- Click on panadapter → dial moves to clicked frequency (existing behavior, unchanged).
- Press-and-drag on panadapter or waterfall → spectrum window pans, dial stays on its signal.
- Touch drag works on mobile/tablet.
- HL2 bench-tested on 28400 (10m, only resonant antenna) before merge.
notes: Spec: docs/prd/panfall_behavior.md (committed alongside implementation). Brainstormed 2026-05-23 — drag = pure visual viewport offset over the radio's IQ sample bandwidth (~19.2 kHz at low rate); retune fires only on release when viewport edge falls outside sample window, with directional landing (pan up → new sample window's bottom edge = viewport bottom; pan down → new sample top = viewport top). Dial (vfoHz) never moves on drag.

title: Persist TX Leveler max-gain and Mic Gain to LiteDB
description:
TX Leveler max-gain and Mic Gain settings revert to defaults on every restart. They live only in frontend localStorage today and are re-POSTed on connect — see comment in zeus-web/src/components/ConnectPanel.tsx:126: 'micGainDb / levelerMaxGainDb don't have a server-authoritative path yet, so they continue to push from localStorage. Migrating them to the same StateDto pattern is the natural follow-up.'

In desktop mode (Photino) localStorage does not reliably survive between launches, so both controls reset to their hardcoded defaults each session.

Investigated 2026-05-24:
- Volume / RX AF (RxAfGainDb) is ALREADY persisted server-side via RadioStateStore. Dumped zeus-prefs.db and confirmed last operator value (1.0 dB) is on disk. No change needed there. If AF still appears to revert, repro together — different bug.
- TX Leveler max gain: SetTxLevelerMaxGain only pushes to WDSP (DspPipelineService.CurrentEngine), nothing reads/writes LiteDB.
- TX Mic Gain: same — direct push to WDSP, no persistence.

Implementation:
- Add TxLevelerMaxGainDb and TxMicGainDb to RadioStateEntry (defaults match current WDSP defaults: leveler 8, mic 0).
- Add the two fields to StateDto.
- RadioService: hydrate from RadioStateStore at construction; add SetTxLevelerMaxGain / SetTxMicGain that Mutate state and trigger debounce flush.
- DspPipelineService: apply on change and on channel open, with _applied* caching.
- ZeusEndpoints: /api/tx/leveler-max-gain and /api/tx/mic-gain route through RadioService instead of pushing straight to the engine.
- Frontend: read levelerMaxGainDb + micGainDb from RadioStateDto; drop the localStorage-on-connect re-POSTs in ConnectPanel.tsx applyPostConnectEffects.
- Test: extend RadioStateStoreTests with leveler + mic-gain round-trip.
notes: AF (RxAfGainDb) verified already persisted — see RadioStateStoreTests + dumped zeus-prefs.db row showing 1.0.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #471 (fix/persist-leveler-micgain): mic gain + Leveler max-gain now persist server-side via RadioStateStore. Volume (RxAfGainDb) was already wired correctly — disk snapshot confirmed.

title: Rotator desktop autostart + LiteDB unification (bottom bar shows connected while Settings unselected)
description:
On fresh desktop launch the bottom-bar rotator pill shows 'Connected' to 127.0.0.1, but the Settings panel shows the rotator as unselected. Two coupled bugs:

1. Rotator state is mirrored in browser localStorage (zeus-web/src/state/rotator-store.ts) and the frontend re-POSTs the cached config on page load, which kicks the backend into 'connect to 127.0.0.1' on startup even when the operator never enabled it.
2. Settings reads from a different source of truth, so the two surfaces disagree.

Fix: collapse to LiteDB-only persistence on the backend. Remove the localStorage mirror + auto-POST from rotator-store.ts. Backend should hydrate Enabled/host/port from LiteDB at startup, and only auto-connect on launch if the operator had it enabled at the previous clean exit. Bottom-bar pill and Settings panel must read the same store.

See memory: project_rotator_resume.md.
notes: PR #469 → develop (https://github.com/Kb2uka/openhpsdr-zeus/pull/469). Awaiting bench verification on next desktop launch (LiteDB Enabled=false should stay off; flipping Enabled + restart should auto-reconnect with no client POST).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #469 (fix(rotator): collapse to LiteDB as sole source of truth) — already merged to develop as 0b51919. Identical change had been queued locally; verified by empty git diff against the merged commit.

title: Anvelina-PRO3 not autodetected — operator must manually override variant
description:
Tester reports Anvelina-PRO3 is not detected as the AnvelinaPro3 variant of OrionMkII; they have to manually push/override the variant in PreferredRadioStore. When left to autodetect the board comes up as ANAN-200D (or some other 0x0A-family default), so the SupportsAnvelinaDxOc capability stays false and the DX OC columns never appear in PA Settings.

This is separate from zeus-jgp (the OC-lines bug). Even with the variant set manually, OC 1-7 still doesn't work — that's the wire-encoding side. THIS bead is just about discovery/variant resolution.

The 0x0A wire byte is shared across G2 / G2_1K / 7000DLE / 8000DLE / OrionMkII / AnvelinaPro3 / RedPitaya. Discovery returns 0x0A and we then need a secondary signal to disambiguate which variant. Today the user has to pick manually in the UI.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Identify what signal in Protocol 2 discovery (or post-connect handshake) can disambiguate AnvelinaPro3 from other 0x0A family members
- [ ] Wire the disambiguation into RadioService.EffectiveOrionMkIIVariant so an Anvelina-PRO3 user sees DX OC columns without manual override
- [ ] Preserve manual override (user can still pick a different variant in the UI if discovery is wrong)
- [ ] Tester confirms autodetect works on their Anvelina-PRO3
- [ ] Don't regress detection on G2 / 7000DLE / 8000DLE etc.
notes:
Reported by tester via Brian, 2026-05-23, during zeus-jgp OC-lines investigation. Tester quote: 'PRO3 wasn't detected I push manually and override. But even if I allow autodetection as 200D, OC lines doesn't work.'

Where to look:
- Zeus.Protocol2 discovery code (search Discover / DiscoveryResponse / DiscoveryAdvert)
- Zeus.Contracts/OrionMkIIVariant.cs — current variant resolution
- Zeus.Server.Hosting/RadioService.EffectiveOrionMkIIVariant — read path
- Zeus.Server.Hosting/PreferredRadioStore — where the manual override lives
- piHPSDR's discovery code for Saturn family — they may have an MCU-string or firmware-ID we're ignoring

title: OC (Open Collector) lines not working — Anvelina + OC 1-7 on other boards
description:
OC (Open Collector) output lines are not driving correctly across the board lineup. Confirmed broken on Anvelina-PRO3; OC 1-7 also reported non-functional on other boards. Operators use OC lines for band-switched accessory control (amps, filters, antenna switches, transverters) so this gates real-world station integration.

Scope:
- Anvelina-PRO3: full OC line set
- All other supported boards (Hermes, ANAN-10/100/100B/100D/200D, OrionMkII family, HermesII, HermesC10, HL2): OC 1-7

The fix needs a board-by-board audit against MW0LGE Thetis (the authoritative reference per CLAUDE.md). Wire-byte layout for OC lines differs across protocol families and per-board capability sets; we should not assume one shape fits all.
design:
Likely lives at three seams: (1) protocol packet construction in Zeus.Protocol1/Zeus.Protocol2 where OC bytes are placed in the C&C/control bytes, (2) BoardCapabilitiesTable + per-board variant routing (HermesLite2DriveProfile pattern) since some boards expose 7 vs 8 OC lines and OrionMkII variants differ, (3) LiteDB persistence for per-band OC assignments. Frontend needs an OC matrix UI similar to Thetis's OC-by-band table.

Anti-pattern to avoid: hard-coding OC byte layout per board inside the TX/control path. Add an IRadioOcProfile (mirroring IRadioDriveProfile) so each board's quirks live in one place.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Full audit of OC-line handling in MW0LGE Thetis (per protocol family + per board)
- [ ] Documented mapping: which Thetis surface drives OC, which wire bits/bytes, per-band persistence, TX vs RX OC behaviour
- [ ] Compare against Zeus: identify exactly where Zeus diverges (missing send, wrong byte position, wrong board gating, no persistence, no UI)
- [ ] Plan covering: backend (P1/P2 client OC encoding), Zeus.Contracts (if a DTO is needed), persistence (LiteDB), per-board variant handling, frontend OC settings panel
- [ ] OC 1-7 functional on at least one non-HL2 board (bench-verifiable by maintainer)
- [ ] OC lines functional on Anvelina-PRO3
- [ ] HL2 sanity-checked (don't regress what works)
notes:
## 2026-05-23 — Diagnostic logging added (uncommitted, local develop)

Added three log emissions to capture the OC data flow end-to-end:

1. **RadioService.RecomputePaAndPush** — extended existing `pa.recompute` log to include `ocTx`/`ocRx`/`ocDxTx`/`ocDxRx` read from PaSettingsStore for the current band. Proves what was looked up.
2. **Protocol1Client.TxLoopAsync** — extended existing 1Hz `p1.tx.rate` log to include current `_ocTxMask`/`_ocRxMask` + `mox`. Proves what the wire builder will see.
3. **Protocol2Client.SendCmdHighPriority** — new edge-triggered `p2.cmd_hp.tx` log printing board+variant, all four masks, and the actual byte values written to `p[1401]` (standard OC) and `p[1397]` (Anvelina DX OC). Proves what hit the wire.

Tests: 740 passed / 0 failed. Build clean.

Symptom from tester (2026-05-23 via Brian): 'no pin ever goes high' on any board, AND Anvelina-PRO3 doesn't even autodetect (filed separately as [[zeus-xzk]]).

Next step for maintainer:
- Pull develop, run, set OC TX bit 1 on the current band, key MOX briefly (28400 only — see project memory)
- Grep server logs for `pa.recompute`, `p1.tx.rate`, `p2.cmd_hp.tx`
- Send the three log lines back — that localises the bug to one of:
  a) RecomputePaAndPush sees 0 → UI-to-store persistence bug
  b) RecomputePaAndPush sees non-zero, p1.tx.rate sees 0 → SetOcMasks not propagating
  c) Both see non-zero, but pin still doesn't go high → wire-level (polarity / firmware / hardware)

Linked: [[zeus-xzk]] for the Anvelina detection issue.

title: bug: SetVfo with CTUN on doesn't auto-recenter when shift exceeds IF capacity (root cause of #461)
description:
When CTUN is on and SetVfo is called with a frequency whose shift from RadioLoHz exceeds the WDSP IF capacity (~±sampleRate*0.46 Hz), Zeus leaves RadioLoHz frozen and asks the WDSP shift stage to move the IF by an impossible amount. Result: radio stays on the old band, audio silent, panadapter centred on the old NCO, big VFO digits showing the new dial. Operator perception: 'frequency display is wrong'.

The bug bites hardest in TCI/CAT digital-mode workflows where band changes (e.g. 15m FT8 21.074 → 20m FT8 14.080) are routine — a 7 MHz shift can't possibly fit in a ±88 kHz IF at 192 kHz sample rate.

Thetis handles this in txtVFOAFreq_LostFocus (console.cs:32189-32257): when the proposed shift exceeds sample_rate_rx1 * 0.92 / 2, it re-centres CentreFrequency to the new dial and zeros rx1_osc — i.e. retunes the radio and snaps the panadapter. CTUN therefore only freezes the panadapter for SMALL movements that the WDSP shift can actually accomodate; band changes / memory recalls always fall through to a hardware retune.

Zeus omitted this auto-recenter check when issue #427 introduced CTUN.
acceptance_criteria: On a P1 or P2 radio with CTUN on, calling /api/vfo (or sending TCI vfo:...) with a frequency that produces a shift larger than sampleRate*0.46 from RadioLoHz must (a) update RadioLoHz to the new dial, (b) push the new frequency to the radio hardware, (c) update the panadapter centre. The radio physically retunes, audio resolves. Small CTUN movements (shift within IF capacity) keep current behaviour: panadapter frozen, WDSP shift, no hardware retune. Verified end-to-end against the TCI 15m→20m FT8 repro from GH #461.
notes:
Related: zeus-96e (visual CTUN indicator — added as debug aid during this investigation, will be reverted once this bug is fixed per maintainer's preference).

Original CTUN implementation: issue #427.

Thetis reference: txtVFOAFreq_LostFocus at console.cs:32105+, especially the IF-out-of-range branch at 32243-32257.

Maintainer concern (CLAUDE.md): operator-visible behaviour change. However, the change is to MATCH Thetis behaviour (which CLAUDE.md cites as the authoritative reference), so this is a 'Protocol / DSP compliance fix where Zeus diverges from Thetis' — green-light per CLAUDE.md autonomous-agent boundaries.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #463 — SetVfo now mirrors Thetis CTUN safeguards (auto-recenter on out-of-visible-span/out-of-IF, fromExternal=true bypass for CAT/TCI/calibration callers). Verified locally against UI band-button repro from GH #461 plus TCI FT8→FT4 sub-band hop.

title: Small screen / low resolution hides controls
description:
When the viewport is too small (low resolution or narrow window), some controls become inaccessible — they get pushed off-screen, clipped, or hidden behind other panels with no scroll/overflow affordance to reach them.

Observed: at constrained resolutions the operator cannot find/reach certain controls in the workspace and top bar.

Acceptance:
- Identify which panels/controls disappear or clip at common smaller resolutions (e.g. 1366x768, 1280x800, narrow laptop windows).
- Provide a way to reach every control at the minimum supported resolution: overflow menus, scroll regions, responsive collapse, or a documented minimum window size.
- Do not change the Hermes Lite 2 panel aesthetic or token palette (see CLAUDE.md visual-design red-light).
- Do not change defaults or control behavior — only reachability.
acceptance_criteria: Minimum supported resolution is 1366x768. At 1366x768 every control in the top bar, workspace panels, and bottom bar must be reachable without clipping or off-screen overflow — via responsive layout, overflow menus, or scroll regions. No control may be permanently hidden at this resolution. Hermes Lite 2 panel aesthetic and token palette must be preserved (no visual-design changes). Defaults and control behavior unchanged — reachability only.
notes: PR #487 closed unmerged 2026-05-24 — CSS-only overflow approach (`overflow-x: auto` on .transport + .topbar, `flex-shrink: 0` on children) did not produce the desired result when verified at 1366×768 in browser. Approach abandoned. Next attempt should consider responsive collapse / kebab-overflow menus or a redesigned layout rather than scroll affordances. Branch `claude/zeus-8tz-small-screen-reach` preserved on origin for reference.

title: PS engine arms on non-HL2 Protocol 1 boards, freezing audio + waterfall
description:
Reported in GH #426 against Hermes on v0.8.2: enabling PS on the front-panel button freezes both audio and the waterfall.

Wire-level gating is already in place:
- Zeus.Protocol1/Protocol1Client.cs:643 — NumReceiversMinusOne only bumps when (psOn && isHl2 && moxOn). Correct.
- Zeus.Protocol1/Protocol1Client.cs:1004 — PS 4-DDC parser path only engages on HermesLite2. Correct.

But engine-level gating leaks:
- Zeus.Server.Hosting/DspPipelineService.cs:687-694 — engine.SetPsEnabled(true) runs for ANY board, with a Task.Delay(100).Wait() on the state-change thread.

On a non-HL2 P1 board, WDSP's PS state machine is told to arm with no possible feedback path. It sits in COLLECT waiting on paired samples that never arrive. Combined with the 100 ms blocking wait on the state-change thread, this matches the freeze symptom in linoobs's screenshot.
design: Add a Board == HermesLite2 gate around engine.SetPsEnabled(true) in DspPipelineService.cs:687-694 for the P1 path (P2 ANAN-class PS is separate). Optionally surface PS-not-supported state via /api/radio/capabilities so the frontend can disable the PS button on non-HL2 P1 boards.
acceptance_criteria: On Hermes (and any non-HL2 P1 board), enabling PS does not freeze audio or waterfall. Either PS is gated off entirely (UI shows 'not supported on this board') OR the engine arm is skipped while the wire stays at single-DDC. PS on HL2 is unaffected.
notes: Discovered alongside the codec bug in GH #426. The wire bits are already board-gated, just the engine arm leaks. Low-risk one-liner.
close_reason: Fixed by PR #435 (commit dd11d88) — gates engine SetPsEnabled on HL2, skips 100ms blocking settle on non-HL2 P1 boards. Merged 2026-05-21.

title: Sound device selection in Zeus UI — input + output device pickers (enables HL2+ front-panel + Square SDR 2 + paves way for cmASIO/low-latency work)
description:
Operators currently can't choose which OS audio device Zeus uses for mic input / RX output — it just takes the OS default. This blocks:

- #243: HL2+ front-panel mic/phones (WA3O) and Square SDR 2 front-panel input (41south) — operators have a USB codec on the radio but can't route Zeus to it without making it the OS default
- #248: low-latency audio work — selecting a specific low-latency / cmASIO-bridged device is a prerequisite

Brian's commitment on #243: 'days, not weeks'.

Scope:
- Add device picker to the Audio settings panel (frontend): input device (mic) + output device (RX playback)
- Server-side: select getUserMedia constraint by deviceId for mic; setSinkId() on the <audio> element for output
- Persist selected device IDs to LiteDB so they survive reload (with graceful fallback if device disappears)
- See #83 for prior design prompt — claude's old completion comment has a usable spec

Related work / dependencies:
- #83 — pre-existing audio tab design (frontend skeleton)
- #248 — low-latency / cmASIO discussion (downstream consumer)
- #290 — Analog Audio request (will likely be answered by the same UI)

Ping list when shipped: WA3O, 41south on #243; JakeGT68 on #248.
notes: External: https://github.com/Kb2uka/openhpsdr-zeus/issues/243 — same UI also unblocks #248 and #290

title: Fix Band Volts toggle on Square SDR 2 / HL2-class radios — audit deskHPSDR/Thetis/piHPSDR for sibling wire-write quirks while at it
description:
Root cause confirmed in KB2UKA-Agent comment on #361: Zeus and deskHPSDR write the same wire bit (C3 bit 3 = LT2208_DITHER_ON). deskHPSDR gates it on have_dither=1 for both DEVICE_HERMES_LITE and DEVICE_HERMES_LITE2 (radio.c:1547-1556); any HL2-class radio drives the bit unconditionally if the operator's checkbox is on. Zeus's gating is stricter and misses Square SDR 2.

Scope (1-line patch + audit):
1. Loosen the Zeus gate so any HL2-class board (HermesLite, HermesLite2, Square SDR 2 — i.e. discovery-byte that maps to the HL2 family) drives the bit when Band Volts checkbox is on.
2. While in the code path, audit deskHPSDR + MW0LGE Thetis + piHPSDR for any sibling toggles on this same wire that Zeus may also be mis-gating — don't ship a one-bit patch that misses a related one.
3. Bench-test on HL2 (Brian) — confirm no regression on the boards Zeus already handles.
4. 41south offered to test the build on Square SDR 2 — ping #361 when there's a binary.

Files likely touched: Zeus.Protocol1/Protocol1Client.cs (frame builder for C3), Zeus.Server.Hosting/BoardCapabilitiesTable.cs (if a capability flag belongs here).
notes: External: https://github.com/Kb2uka/openhpsdr-zeus/issues/361 — followup to #279

comments.author: Brian Keating
comments.text:
## Audit findings — wire-diff settled, root cause is hardware-architectural

**Wire-diff** (between Zeus and deskHPSDR on the C3 byte): no fix needed.
- Zeus's `ControlFrame.WriteConfigPayload` drives C3 bit 3 (`LT2208_DITHER_ON`) on every Config-register frame when `EnableHl2BandVolts=true`. There is no board gate on the wire side.
- Square SDR 2 classifies as `HermesLite2` per @41south's log (fw=75.52); `HasHl2OptionalToggles` is true, the RADIO tab and checkbox render. Confirmed by 41south.
- The bit Zeus writes is identical to what deskHPSDR writes (`LT2208_DITHER_ON = 0x08` at output_buffer[C3]) and to what mi0bot Thetis writes via `NetworkIO.SetADCDither`.

**Sibling C3-byte audit** (deskHPSDR + mi0bot Thetis):
- **bit 2 / LT2208_RANDOM** — exposed by mi0bot Thetis as `chkHL2PsSync → SetADCRandom` (HL2 Power Supply Sync), and by deskHPSDR per-receiver `random`. **Zeus does not expose this.** Same-byte sibling toggle that fits naturally next to Band Volts on the RADIO panel.
- **bit 3 + audio codec** — deskHPSDR forces the DITHER bit on when `hl2_audio_codec=1` (rare HL2 variant with companion audio codec). Low priority.
- bits [1:0], bit 5, bits [7:5] — already handled correctly in Zeus or N/A.

**Square SDR 2 — the actual root cause** (from the vendor archive, now mirrored in `docs/references/radios/square-sdr-2/` on branch `fix/square-sdr-2-band-volts`):

The board has a separate STM32G031 microcontroller that owns the front-panel band-voltage / BCD / fan-control outputs. Its gateware variant (`variants/hl2b5up_SquareSDR2/hermeslite.v`) instantiates `hermeslite_core` with **both** non-default parameters enabled:

```verilog
hermeslite_core #(
  .UART (1),
  .FAN  (1), // FAN must be 1 for PA thermal protection to work (WiSER)
) hermeslite_core_i ( ... )
```

That switches on two independent band-voltage paths:

| Path | Driven by | Zeus action |
|---|---|---|
| A. FPGA `fan_pwm` pin band-voltage | C3 bit 3 (`EnableHl2BandVolts`) | already shipped, PR #314 |
| B. Rear-panel BVO PWM + BCD via STM32 | FPGA `extamp` emits Kenwood `FA0000XXXXXXXX;` over `io_uart_txd` → STM32 USART2 → STM32 drives PWM | **none — already works** as long as Zeus writes the TX-VFO register, which it does on every register rotation |

If the XPA125B is hooked to the rear-panel BVO (Path B), the amp should follow Zeus's frequency changes today with no toggle. That's the "default-on" behavior @41south saw under deskHPSDR — deskHPSDR isn't doing anything special, the FPGA just emits CAT on every freq update and the STM32 handles the rest.

If the XPA125B is hooked to a case-fan-style header (Path A), the Band Volts checkbox is the right control and the bit is already on the wire.

**Recommended next step:** ask @41south which connector the XPA125B is on (rear-panel BVO vs. case-fan), and either add a 1-Hz diagnostic log of the C3 byte (Path A) or of TX-freq-register writes (Path B) in the next build so we can confirm what's actually reaching his radio.

Full audit + provenance in `docs/references/radios/square-sdr-2/{README,SOURCE}.md` (commit `9400133` on `fix/square-sdr-2-band-volts`).

title: installers: codesign fails on Contents/MacOS/ data subdirs (BandPlans, wwwroot)
description:
## Context

Tried to add a `sign_and_notarize.sh` step for the macOS build so the DMG produced by `installers/create-macos-app.sh` can ship Developer-ID-signed + notarized. Got blocked by a structural mismatch between the current bundle layout and Apple's codesign rules.

Mirrored from GitHub issue: https://github.com/Kb2uka/openhpsdr-zeus/issues/389
Original reporter: rampa069 (Ramon Martinez)

## What happens

Final bundle sign step (`codesign --force --sign "Developer ID ..." --options runtime --entitlements Zeus.entitlements --timestamp Zeus.app`) fails with:

```
/.../Zeus.app: code object is not signed at all
In subcomponent: /.../Zeus.app/Contents/MacOS/BandPlans/EI.segments.json
```

Same error happens whether or not nested dylibs / `OpenhpsdrZeus` / `calculus` / `createdump` are pre-signed first. `xattr -lr` shows no stale signature attrs on the JSON files — the issue is structural, not residual.

## Root cause

`create-macos-app.sh` copies the entire `dotnet publish` output straight into `Contents/MacOS/`. That places three data subdirectories inside what Apple considers the *code* directory:

- `Contents/MacOS/BandPlans/` (8 region JSON files)
- `Contents/MacOS/wwwroot/` (the Vite-built SPA)
- `Contents/MacOS/runtimes/` (per-RID native libs — this one actually contains code, so it's fine once the dylibs inside are pre-signed)

codesign walks subdirectories of `Contents/MacOS/` looking for code subcomponents and rejects the bundle as soon as it finds a non-Mach-O file in one. There is no supported flag that disables this behaviour in current macOS (`--resource-rules` was removed; `CFBundleResourceSpec` no longer honoured). Apple's bundle conventions put data under `Contents/Resources/`, not `Contents/MacOS/`.

## Why a launcher-script chdir doesn't help

Initial thought was: leave files where they are, have `launch.sh` chdir to `Resources/` if we move them there. That doesn't work because the backend hardcodes the lookup path:

- `Zeus.Server.Hosting/ZeusHost.cs:54` — `ContentRootPath = AppContext.BaseDirectory`
- `Zeus.Server.Hosting/ZeusHost.cs:360` — `Directory.SetCurrentDirectory(baseDir)` (this explicitly overrides any chdir launch.sh did)
- `Zeus.Server.Hosting/BandPlanStore.cs:115-116` — `Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, "BandPlans")`

`AppContext.BaseDirectory` = directory of `OpenhpsdrZeus`, currently `Contents/MacOS/`. So merely moving the data files breaks the app at startup unless either the runtime layout puts everything in the same directory as the binary, or the lookup paths become configurable.

## Resolution options for maintainer review

Both keep `Contents/MacOS/` clean of data subdirs so codesign passes — they differ in invasiveness:

1. **Tiny native shim, no .NET code change.** Compile a ~20-line C launcher as `Contents/MacOS/zeus-launcher` (the `CFBundleExecutable`). The whole `dotnet publish` output (including `BandPlans/`, `wwwroot/`, `zetaHat.bin`, `OpenhpsdrZeus`, `runtimes/`, all managed dlls) moves to `Contents/Resources/app/`. The shim does `chdir("../Resources/app")`, pins `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH`, then `execv("./OpenhpsdrZeus", ...)`. Because `AppContext.BaseDirectory` ends up = `Resources/app/`, every hardcoded path keeps working. Server-mode wrapper (`openhpsdr-zeus-server`) gets the same treatment. `create-macos-app.sh` change only — no `.cs` edits.

2. **Env-var-driven path overrides in the backend.** Add `ZEUS_CONTENT_ROOT`, `ZEUS_BANDPLANS_DIR`, `ZEUS_WDSP_DATA_DIR` reads in ZeusHost / BandPlanStore with fallback to `AppContext.BaseDirectory` so non-bundle dev runs are unaffected. `launch.sh` exports them pointing at `Contents/Resources/` and the data files move there in `create-macos-app.sh`. Touches three .cs files, fully back-compat outside the bundle.

Picking (1) avoids any production-code change, at the cost of carrying a small C launcher in the repo. Picking (2) keeps the launcher as a shell script but spends three small additions in the host code.

## Draft work

WIP signing script (`installers/sign_and_notarize.sh`) and entitlements file (`installers/Zeus.entitlements`) are on local branch `rampa/macos-codesign-script` (not pushed). The signing flow itself is solid and verified up to the point of the structural rejection — both the layout-fix options above slot in without further changes to the signing step. Happy to push the branch and open a PR once direction is picked.

## Files affected by either fix

- `installers/create-macos-app.sh` (layout + launcher swap)
- `installers/sign_and_notarize.sh` (new, draft)
- `installers/Zeus.entitlements` (new, draft)
- Option 2 also: `Zeus.Server.Hosting/ZeusHost.cs`, `Zeus.Server.Hosting/BandPlanStore.cs`

## Environment

- macOS 15.x (Darwin 25.5.0) on Apple Silicon
- Apple Developer ID signing cert imported, notary profile configured
- Build of branch `develop` (no changes), `Zeus.app` produced by `./create-macos-app.sh 0.7.6 arm64`
notes:
## Update 2026-05-25 (rampa069) — branch fix/macos-codesign-no-deep, commit 2d043e0

Re-evaluated the --deep masking. Empirically confirmed (ad-hoc inside-out
signing) that codesign without --deep treats EVERY file under Contents/MacOS/
as a code subcomponent — it rejects not just the data subdirs but the flat
managed .dll and even .deps.json. So issue Option 2 (env-var data relocation)
CANNOT drop --deep on its own; the ~323 managed DLLs in MacOS/ still trip it.

Implemented Option 1 (no C shim needed — launch.sh script as CFBundleExecutable):
move the entire publish to Contents/Resources/app/, leave only launch.sh in
Contents/MacOS/. Files under Resources/ seal as resources; the bundle signs
inside-out with no --deep (apphost + ~17 dylibs hardened-runtime + timestamp,
then the bundle). launch.sh cd's to Resources/app so BaseDirectory + WDSP
fopen resolve unchanged. Kept ZEUS_WEBROOT/ZEUS_BANDPLANS_DIR overrides in
ZeusHost/BandPlanStore (fallback to binary dir) as an explicit pin.

Verified locally: structure, functional launch (HTTP 200, wwwroot + BandPlans
+ WDSP NR2 models all load), and ad-hoc codesign --verify --deep --strict
passes on both .app bundles with NO --deep. Build + full test suite green.

PENDING ACCEPTANCE: real Developer-ID sign + Apple notarization via
~/zeus-sign-notarize.sh (needs the cert/notary account) — confirm notarytool
Accepted + staple + spctl WITHOUT --deep before closing.
close_reason: macOS bundle signs + notarizes WITHOUT --deep. Fix: payload → Contents/Resources/app/, launch.sh stub in MacOS/, inside-out signing. Branch fix/macos-codesign-no-deep (commit 2d043e0). Notarization Accepted + stapled + spctl OK on real Developer-ID build.

title: Retire legacy 'cw' and 'cwdecoder' panels + migrate saved layouts to 'cw-station'
description:
Follow-up to the CW station panel work (introduces 'cw-station', merging keyer + decoder terminal + threshold scope). That PR intentionally KEEPS the legacy 'cw' (keyer) and 'cwdecoder' (decoder) panel IDs registered for backwards-compatibility with saved layouts. Once the new panel is accepted and visually signed off by the maintainer, remove the two legacy panels.

Scope:
- Remove the legacy 'cw' and 'cwdecoder' registrations from zeus-web/src/layout/panels.ts and delete their now-unused components.
- Add a layout migration: any saved workspace layout referencing 'cw' or 'cwdecoder' must be mapped to 'cw-station' (or de-duplicated if both were present) on load, so existing users don't lose the panel.
- Decide and document the dedup behaviour when a saved layout contains both legacy panels.

Gated on: the cw-station PR landing first. Do not start until the unified approach is confirmed by the maintainer (open question in that PR is whether to merge the two panels at all).
acceptance_criteria: Legacy 'cw'/'cwdecoder' IDs removed; saved layouts referencing them load into 'cw-unified' without error or panel loss; dedup behaviour documented.
notes: Red-light-adjacent: removes operator-facing panels + changes layout behaviour, so needs maintainer awareness. Keep separate from the unified-panel PR per CONTRIBUTING 'one feature per PR'.

title: CW sidetone monitor + audio routing (PR 4 of 4)
description:
Fourth PR for [[zeus-4np]]. When the radio is keying CW — either via the engine ([[zeus-drf]]) or via the hardware key ([[zeus-xcz]]) — the operator should hear sidetone in Zeus's audio out, not only on the HL2's headphone jack.

## Scope

- WDSP TXA has a sidetone generator (CWSetSidetoneFreq / CWSetSidetoneVolume / CWSetGain depending on the WDSP API surface — confirm against the loaded native build before committing). Verify whether sidetone routes through the same TXA output we already capture, or needs a separate tap.
- DspPipelineService routes RXA → IRxAudioSink today. While MOX is on, RX is muted. Add a sidetone path so CWX/hardware-key drives the same sink with the sidetone tone.
- Frontend slider: sidetone gain (dB) and pitch (Hz). Persist via CwSettingsStore from [[zeus-o27]] (fields already reserved: SidetoneGainDb, SidetoneHz).
- Sidetone-on-hardware-key: when [[zeus-xcz]]'s ExternalPttService claims MOX, also kick the sidetone generator. Release on the same falling-edge path.

## Open questions to answer in the PR

- Does WDSP emit sidetone into the TX audio chain or into a separate buffer? If separate, where does it route on the operator's headphones today vs over the wire to the HL2 (HL2 has no codec, so it can't be the radio's headphone jack — must be host-side audio).
- For hardware-key CW the host has no key timing; sidetone would need to follow C0[2]/C0[0] edges from the inbound packet (effectively sample-rate-paced, not packet-paced). This may push the design toward host-side keying-detection logic in the DSP thread, not an event in ExternalPttService.

## Acceptance

Operator hears CW sidetone in Zeus when keying CW via macro send, TCI cw_msg, or external HL2 key. Gain and pitch sliders work and persist.
acceptance_criteria: Audible sidetone in Zeus's audio out for every CW-keying path (UI macro send, TCI cw_msg, hardware key). Gain/pitch sliders adjust live and survive restart. No artefacts during MOX on/off transitions.
notes:
PR #508 open against develop (branch feature/cw-sidetone-monitor, commit 2875e0f).

Done: host-side CwSidetoneSource (WDSP ships no sidetone API) — phase accumulator + 5 ms raised-cosine attack/release matching the on-air CW envelope. DspPipelineService.Tick mixes it additively into the RX audio buffer pre-publish, so all sinks (browser WS, native, TCI audio_stream) get it; the existing MOX fade silences RXA while keying so only the tone is heard. Keying sources: CwEngine.PlayJobAsync per-symbol (macro + cw_msg); ExternalPttService on hardware-key edges, gated to CWU/CWL. Pitch 200-1200 Hz + gain -60..0 dB persist via CwSettingsStore; PUT /api/cw/settings pushes live to the generator (no restart). PITCH/SIDE sliders in CW panel (reuse cw-wpm-* classes, no new tokens). 12 CwSidetoneSource tests; build 0/0; front 229 green.

Bench-verified (operator): macro send, cw_msg, AND external hardware key all produce audible sidetone in CWU/CWL; sliders adjust live; values persist across restart.

Follow-up: TCI raw-key path (keyer:1 -> CwEngine.PlayRawKeyAsync, which lives in #504/zeus-j3t) needs one Down()/Up() pair added once #504 merges — extends sidetone to the 4th keying path.

Pre-existing unrelated failures seen locally: 4 ZeroBeat* tests are red on clean develop too — not caused by this work.

title: TCI keyer hookup (PR 3 of 4)
description:
Third PR for [[zeus-4np]]. Replace the stub at TciSession.cs:595-601 (the four-case 'no CW engine yet' fallthrough) with real calls into the engine landed in [[zeus-drf]].

## Scope

- TCI commands to wire:
  - cw_macros_speed — sets WPM for cw_macros / cw_msg invocations
  - cw_macros n — transmit macro slot n (1-based)
  - cw_msg text — transmit arbitrary text
  - keyer 1 / 0 — manual key down / up (engine.RawKeyAsync(bool) — minimal surface, NOT a text send)
- Resolve cw_macros against the same CwSettingsStore used by the UI ([[zeus-o27]]), so the macros operators edit in Zeus's panel are the ones any external TCI client sees.
- Report status back via TCI: cw_macros_empty (when stop fires), MOX/Tx events already wired.
- Unit tests for the four command parsers and the engine.RawKeyAsync path.

## Acceptance

A TCI client implementing the CW commands (contest logger / SDR console — exact program out of scope) sending cw_msg 'CQ TEST' transmits at the current WPM. Invoking cw_macros N transmits the macro the operator edited in the Zeus UI.
acceptance_criteria: A TCI client implementing the CW commands can drive cw_msg, cw_macros, cw_macros_speed and keyer end-to-end via the engine without going through the UI path. No regressions in existing TCI tests.
notes:
PR #504 open against develop (branch feature/cw-tci-keyer-hookup, commit c36b48b).

Done: cw_msg / cw_macros / cw_macros_speed / keyer routed through CwEngine; macros resolve against CwSettingsStore (same as UI macro pad); cw_macros_speed mutates the persisted WPM the UI binds. CwEngine.RawKeyAsync(bool,int?,ct) added for keyer:rx,bool[,dur] with selective cancel (keyer:0 only aborts an in-flight raw-key, not a parallel text send). TciServer broadcasts cw_macros_empty on Sending->Idle. New TciCwParser isolates wire-format edge cases (trailing repeat on cw_msg, slot bounds, optional keyer duration). 29 parser + 5 raw-key tests; build 0/0; 1294 suite green.

Verified via websocket (websocat ws://127.0.0.1:40001) — handshake, echoes, cw_macros_empty arrive; MOX gating refuses cleanly with no radio. NOT yet tested with a full TCI logger client.

Out of scope (noted in docs/wiki/TCI.md): cw_macros_delay, cw_macros_speed_up/down, cw_macros_stop, cw_terminal, callsign_send, per-RX cw_keyer_speed.

title: CW UI rewire + persisted settings (PR 2 of 4)
description:
Second PR for [[zeus-4np]]. Connect the existing CW Keyer panel to the engine landed in [[zeus-drf]] and persist user-editable state.

## Scope

- Zeus.Server.Hosting/CwSettingsStore.cs — LiteDB-backed, same shape as DspSettingsStore. Fields: Wpm (int, default 22), FarnsworthWpm (int?, default null), Macros (6 slots × string, defaults match the current hardcoded list), SidetoneGainDb (float, default -10), SidetoneHz (int, default = CwDefaults.PitchHz=600).
- GET /api/cw/settings + PUT /api/cw/settings.
- Frontend:
  - zeus-web/src/api/cw.ts — wraps /api/cw/send, /api/cw/abort, /api/cw/settings.
  - CwPanel passes a real onSend that POSTs to /api/cw/send with the current WPM from CwSettingsStore.
  - WPM slider reads/writes the store (not the local useState in App.tsx).
  - Macros editable inline (long-press or 'Edit' toggle), persisted via /api/cw/settings.
  - Queue display reads CwEngineStatus frames from the hub and renders characters in flight (highlight current, fade past).
  - STOP button calls /api/cw/abort.

## Out of scope

- TCI hookup (PR 3).
- Sidetone monitor routing (PR 4 — gain/pitch sliders may land here, but the WS-side routing lands later).
acceptance_criteria: From a clean install the panel shows the persisted macros (defaults on first run), WPM survives a restart, clicking a macro transmits, STOP aborts mid-send. Editing a macro and reloading recovers the edit. No console errors, no TS errors, vitest green.
close_reason: UI rewired to persisted CwSettingsStore (/api/cw/settings PATCH), Telegraph Console redesign landed, WPM slider race fixed, queue display via MsgType 0x30 hub frame. All tests green. Visually validated on bench. zeus-drf dep still open only because PR #477 is in review; commit ed27446 lives on the same branch and its work is complete.

title: QRM eliminator using HL2 dual-antenna inputs (epic)
description:
Epic: investigate and, if feasible, implement a QRM eliminator that uses two antenna inputs on the Hermes-Lite 2 (or on a paired HL2 setup) to null out a local noise source by adaptive phase+amplitude combining (classic MFJ-1026 / X-Phase concept, in DSP).

Scope (driven by the research spike — see child issue):
- A software adaptive canceller running on two synchronised IQ streams (signal antenna + noise antenna).
- Distributed as a Zeus plugin if the architecture permits it; otherwise a core feature behind a board-capability flag.
- UI: a dedicated panel with phase / amplitude / freeze controls and a noise-floor meter showing pre/post cancellation.

Caveats already on the table:
- HL2 declares RxAdcCount=1 in BoardCapabilitiesTable.cs — a single stock HL2 cannot sample two antennas simultaneously. Any real implementation needs either (a) two clock-synced HL2 boards, (b) an HL2 hardware mod that adds a second ADC path, or (c) an external analog canceller controlled by Zeus via a plugin similar to the existing 4O3A device plugins.
- Plugin SDK currently exposes IAudioPlugin for post-DSP audio; an RF/IQ-level extension point does not exist yet. Adding one is a non-trivial architecture change (red-light per CLAUDE.md) and would need maintainer approval.

This epic stays open until the research issue closes with a recommended approach.
acceptance_criteria: Research issue completes with a documented recommendation; epic is then either (a) refined into implementation issues for the recommended approach, or (b) closed with a write-up of why it isn't feasible.
close_reason: Closed without implementation; gated research [[zeus-z8h]] concluded it isn't feasible on stock HL2. Re-open as a new epic if a hardware path (two clock-synced HL2s / external canceller / HW mod) becomes a realistic target.

title: Make panadapter/waterfall split draggable (10–90%), default 50%, persisted in LiteDB
description:
Today the vertical split between the panadapter and the waterfall is hard-coded at 50/50. Operators want to weight one or the other (e.g. more waterfall for digital modes, more panadapter for tuning).

Requirements:
- Default split is 50% (no behaviour change on first launch).
- Drag handle on the boundary; clamp the panadapter share to [10%, 90%] so neither pane can collapse.
- Persist the value in LiteDB (NOT browser localStorage — see project_litedb_tx_filter_persistence.md / project_rotator_resume.md for the same lesson). Backend exposes get/set; frontend reads on mount, writes on drag-end (debounced).
- Per-receiver if we have multiple panadapters, otherwise a single global value is fine for now.

Out of scope: horizontal layout changes, panadapter zoom, waterfall speed.
notes: REVIEW (overnight): approved. Backend PanWfSplitStore mirrors BottomPin pattern (LiteDB, clamp 10..90, default 50, lock-guarded), GET/PUT /api/pan-wf-split registered in ZeusEndpoints + DI in ZeusHost. Frontend pan-wf-split-store.ts has NO localStorage mirror (called out in comments), hydrates on mount, debounced 300 ms persist on drag-end. HeroPanel splitter uses pointer-capture, row-resize cursor, role=separator with proper aria-valuemin/max/now. CSS uses --line / --accent-line tokens only — no palette violations. Both builds + 219 tests green. Close decision correct.
close_reason: Closed

title: ui: VFO display lacks visible CTUN-on indicator (confuses users into 'wrong frequency' reports)
description:
When CTUN is on, the big VFO digits show the dial (VfoHz) while the panadapter centre is anchored to the frozen RadioLoHz. The only CTUN indicator today is the small 'CTUN' button in the transport bar (App.tsx:775-793) gaining .active styling — that button is hide-mobile and easily overlooked.

Result: operators (especially CAT/TCI users) see VFO digits that do not match the panadapter centre and file 'frequency display wrong' bugs (GH #461, plus rampa069 observed the same locally on TCI set-freq with CTUN inadvertently on).

The frequency display is doing exactly what it should — but the design hides the CTUN state so well that the symptom reads as a display bug.
design:
Add a clear visual indicator near the VFO digits (not just the toggle button) when ctunEnabled === true. Options to evaluate with maintainer:

A) Small 'CTUN' badge / icon adjacent to the VFO digits.
B) Subtle tint change on the digits themselves (must use existing tokens — likely a desaturated --accent variant; no new palette additions).
C) Show the frozen RadioLoHz somewhere near the dial so the offset is visible (e.g. dial: 14.200.000 / NCO: 7.050.000).

Whichever is chosen, it must use existing tokens.css variables (no raw hex per maintainer's design rules). Option A is the safest no-design-change path.

Out of scope (red-light per CLAUDE.md): auto-disabling CTUN on CAT/TCI set-freq, or auto-disabling when shift exceeds IF bandwidth. Those would be operator-visible behaviour changes and need maintainer sign-off.
acceptance_criteria: When CTUN is on, an operator scanning the VFO area can tell without clicking that CTUN is active and that the displayed dial differs from the hardware NCO. The fix must not change CTUN semantics, must not auto-toggle CTUN on any wire/TCI/CAT input, and must not introduce raw hex colors (use tokens from zeus-web/src/styles/tokens.css).
notes:
Related GH issue: #461 (frequency display reported as 'not correct' on ANAN G2 — strongly suspected to be CTUN inadvertently on after TCI set-freq).

CTUN spec lives at: RadioService.SetVfo (line 576), RadioService.SetCtun (line 618), DspPipelineService.OnRadioStateChanged (line 548), DspPipelineService panadapter centre selection (line 1541). TCI seam: Tci/TciSession.cs HandleVfo (line 802).

Cross-reference: issue #427 (original CTUN implementation).
close_reason: Abandoned — visual indicator was added as debug aid during investigation, reverted after maintainer (rampa069) confirmed the actual fix should target the missing retune (zeus-0c8 / GH #463), not a UX indicator. With #463 the radio physically retunes on band changes, so the VFO digits / panadapter divergence the badge was meant to surface never occurs.

title: Feature: Waterfall brightness control
description:
Requested in GH #426. Operator finds the waterfall 'too dark' and wants a brightness control.

Red-light per CLAUDE.md (visual design — colors / panadapter WebGL trace are explicitly maintainer-only). The amber #FFA028 trace + waterfall palette is part of the Zeus aesthetic faithful to the Hermes Lite 2 front panel. Any brightness/contrast control is a UX + visual design decision the maintainer owns. Do not implement without maintainer sign-off.
